4. Outros Elementos
Campos de preenchimento opcional
4.1 Informações sobre a performance da organização, em comparação com benchmarks internos (ex: outras
unidades) ou externos (ex: organizações do mesmo setor).
Não foi relatado.
4.2 Descrição de indicadores de emissão de GEE para as atividades da organização. Por exemplo, tCO e/produtos
2
fabricados.
Foram estabelecidos indicadores de emissões relacionados às atividades da empresa, visando a redução de
emissões e melhoria contínua da coleta e manipulação dos dados internos. Os indicadores ligados a consumo de
energia elétrica apresentaram redução significativa em relação ao ano passado, isso porque houve diminuição no
consumo de energia elétrica (cerca de 44% em relação a 2015)
- O consumo de energia elétrica por funcionário do escritório foi de 0,01027 tCO2e
4.3 Descrição de estratégias e projetos para a gestão de emissões de GEE.
Uma vez que grande parte das emissões estão alocadas no escopo 3, a Sustainable Carbon trabalha com algumas
medidas e princípios para diminuir as emissões ao longo do tempo, como por exemplo:
Para fazer o transporte de documentos a empresa prefere utilizar os serviços da empresa EcoBike Courier.
É pratica comum da empresa: realizar reuniões via vídeo conferencia, evitando assim o deslocamento de ida e volta
até o local da reunião.
Quando realiza visita de verificação dos projetos, opta por fazer a visita de um polo inteiro, passando por todos os
projetos do Estado de uma única vez.
4.4 Informações sobre contratos com clientes e fornecedores que incluam cláusulas vinculadas à elaboração de
inventários de GEE e/ou ao envio de informações relacionadas.
Não foi relatado.
4.5 Informações sobre incertezas, exclusões de fontes de dados e outras características da elaboração do
inventário.
A administração do condomínio é responsável pelo controle da energia elétrica adquirida para a área comum do
prédio, essas emissões foram excluídas do inventário de 2016, por dificuldade na coleta de dados e por serem
irrelevantes.
4.6 Descrição sobre ações internas para melhoria da qualidade do inventário de GEE. Por exemplo, sistematização
da coleta de dados, contratação de verificação externa, etc.
A Sustainable Carbon desenvolve sistematização de coleta de dados que abrange todo o escopo de suas atividades
organizacionais, através de ferramentas corporativas e a difusão interna do conhecimento sobre as emissões de
GEE.
Para a coleta interna dos dados, criamos uma plataforma com acesso a todos os colaboradores. Este instrumento
possibilita a coleta dos dados assim que ocorre a atividade e sua emissão, dessa forma facilita o monitoramento
28/06/2022 19:37 10
Inventário 2016 - Sustainable Carbon
dos indicadores, realização do inventário e também o processo de verificação por terceira parte.
Assim como os instrumentos corporativos, o conhecimento compartilhado junto aos colaboradores mostra-se
importante para a qualidade e transparência do inventário. Todos os colaboradores passam por orientações quanto
ao fornecimento dos dados necessários à elaboração do inventário, assim como a realização de treinamentos
internos, visando à difusão de conhecimento sobre os inventários de GEE.
Para obter maior transparência das informações obtidas no inventário, foi estabelecido contrato de prestação de
serviços com uma organização verificadora, acreditada pelo Inmetro, para a validação externa dos dados e
informações do Inventário de GEE.
4.7 Informações sobre a compra de energia elétrica oriunda de fonte renovável.
Não foi relatado.
4.8 Informações sobre autoprodução de energia oriunda de fonte renovável para consumo próprio.
Não foi relatado.
4.9 Informações sobre o estoque de carbono, em toneladas, de sua organização em 31 de dezembro do ano
inventariado.
Não foi relatado.

Declaração de Verificação de inventário de emissões de gases de efeito estufa
Programa Brasileiro GHG Protocol
1
Esta Declaração de Verificação documenta que o Organismo de Verificação (OV) citado abaixo realizou as
atividades de verificação de acordo com as Especificações de Verificação do Programa Brasileiro GHG Protocol
e a norma ABNT NBR ISO 14064-3:2007.
Todos os campos são de preenchimento obrigatório.
Organismo de Verificação (OV) Organização Inventariante (OI)
Nome do OV: Sustainable Carbon – Projetos Ambientais Ltda Nome da OI: RINA BRASIL SERVIÇOS LTDA
Nome do verificador líder: Geisa Principe Nome do responsável pelo inventário: Marcelo Haddad
E-mail: geisa.principe@rina.org E-mail: marcelo@sustainablecarbon.com
As emissões de gases de efeito estufa (GEE) informadas pela Organização Inventariante em seu inventário de
emissões, de 1º de janeiro até 31 de dezembro de 2016, [são verificáveis] e [cumprem os requisitos do
Programa Brasileiro GHG Protocol, detalhados nas Especificações do Programa Brasileiro GHG Protocol de
Contabilização, Quantificação e Publicação de Inventários Corporativos de Emissões de Gases de Efeito Estufa
(EPB).
Nível de Confiança
O Organismo de Verificação (OV) atribuiu o seguinte nível de confiança ao processo de verificação:
Verificação com nível de confiança razoável
“O inventário de gases de efeito estufa da organização inventariante para o ano de [ano] está materialmente
correto, é uma representação justa dos dados e informações de GEE e foi elaborado de acordo com as EPB.”
As limitações do processo de verificação foram:
Verificação com nível de confiança limitado
“Não há indícios de que o inventário de gases de efeito estufa da organização inventariante para o ano de

Inventário 2017 - Sustainable Carbon
SUSTAINABLE CARBON - PROJETOS AMBIENTAIS LTDA.
Nome fantasia: Sustainable Carbon
CNPJ: 09.510.073/0001-06
Setor econômico: Atividades profissionais, científicas e técnicas
Subsetor: Atividades profissionais, científicas e técnicas
Endereço: R DOUTOR BACELAR - 368 - ANDAR 02 Conjunto 23 - Vila Clementino - São
Paulo - SP - 04.026-001
Responsável pela publicação do inventário: Dênis Gonçalves
(denis@sustainablecarbon.com)
Informações institucionais
A Sustainable Carbon é especialista e líder na América Latina no desenvolvimento de
projetos de reduções de emissões e de soluções ligadas ao gerenciamento de Gases de
Efeito Estufa, GEEs.
Nossas soluções permitem que organizações e indivíduos conheçam e reduzam seus
impactos ambientais, possibilitando o equilíbrio das emissões e contribuindo para o
desenvolvimento sustentável de comunidades no mundo todo.
Também conectamos pessoas querendo fazer a diferença no mundo: empresários,
desenvolvedores de projetos, empresas, ONGs, governos, associações e entusiastas de
todo o mundo.
Fazemos isso por meio de mecanismos técnicos e inovadores aprovados pelo mercado de
carbono que quantificam as reduções das emissões GEE. Estabelecendo, assim, um preço
sobre esse serviço ambiental e permitindo o comércio internacional desta commodity
única: o crédito de carbono.
Assim, aqueles que reduzem suas emissões recebem por isso e são capazes de manter os
projetos de carbono, que preservam ecossistemas ameaçados e geram desenvolvimento
na região onde estão localizados.
Somos especialistas no desenvolvimento de projetos de energia renovável, de
substituição de combustível, compostagem, projetos de biomassa e projetos florestais.
Realizamos trabalhos em quase todos os ecossistemas brasileiros. Foi assim que nos
últimos anos a Sustainable Carbon entregou créditos de carbono da mais alta qualidade
para compensações e hoje é a empresa líder neste segmento na América Latina.
Veja abaixo nossas conquistas:
Somos a empresa líder mundial em desenvolvimento de projetos para o mercado
voluntário (VER), gerenciando um portfólio com mais de 50 projetos validados e mais de
16M de toneladas em reduções de emissões;
Pioneira no desenvolvimento de melhores práticas sustentáveis, validamos o primeiro
projeto SOCIALCARBON® em 2008 e o primeiro projeto Gold Standard para o mercado
voluntário no Brasil em 2011;
Mais de 50 comunidades já foram beneficiadas através dos créditos de carbono gerados
para o desenvolvimento sustentável;
Soluções de GEEs para mais de 100 empresas em 23 países.

4. Outros Elementos
Campos de preenchimento opcional
4.1 Informações sobre a performance da organização, em comparação com benchmarks internos (ex: outras
unidades) ou externos (ex: organizações do mesmo setor).
Não foi relatado.
4.2 Descrição de indicadores de emissão de GEE para as atividades da organização. Por exemplo, tCO e/produtos
2
fabricados.
Foram estabelecidos indicadores de emissões relacionados às atividades da empresa, visando a redução de
emissões e melhoria contínua da coleta e manipulação dos dados internos. Os indicadores ligados ao Escopo 3
como: Combustão Estacionária, Emissões Fugitivas, Viagens a negócios, Deslocamento Casa-Trabalho, Esgoto
doméstico gerado, e Resíduos sólidos gerados, quando somados, apresentaram uma redução de 30% nas emissões
com relação à 2016, referente aos mesmos indicadores.
4.3 Descrição de estratégias e projetos para a gestão de emissões de GEE.
Uma vez que grande parte das emissões estão alocadas no escopo 3, a Sustainable Carbon trabalha com algumas
medidas e princípios para diminuir as emissões ao longo do tempo, como por exemplo:
Para fazer o transporte de documentos a empresa prefere utilizar os serviços da empresa EcoBike Courier.
É pratica comum da empresa: realizar reuniões via vídeo conferencia, evitando assim o deslocamento de ida e volta
até o local da reunião.
Quando realiza visita de verificação dos projetos, opta por fazer a visita de um polo inteiro, passando por todos os
projetos do Estado de uma única vez.
4.4 Informações sobre contratos com clientes e fornecedores que incluam cláusulas vinculadas à elaboração de
inventários de GEE e/ou ao envio de informações relacionadas.
Não foi relatado.
4.5 Informações sobre incertezas, exclusões de fontes de dados e outras características da elaboração do
inventário.
A administração do condomínio é responsável pelo controle da energia elétrica adquirida para a área comum do
prédio, essas emissões foram excluídas do inventário de 2017, por dificuldade na coleta de dados e por serem
irrelevantes.
4.6 Descrição sobre ações internas para melhoria da qualidade do inventário de GEE. Por exemplo, sistematização
da coleta de dados, contratação de verificação externa, etc.
A Sustainable Carbon desenvolve sistematização de coleta de dados que abrange todo o escopo de suas atividades
organizacionais, através de ferramentas corporativas e a difusão interna do conhecimento sobre as emissões de
GEE.
Para a coleta interna dos dados, criamos uma plataforma com acesso a todos os colaboradores. Este instrumento
possibilita a coleta dos dados assim que ocorre a atividade e sua emissão, dessa forma facilita o monitoramentodos

Summary:
Sustainable Carbon – Projetos Ambientais Ltda. has commissioned the TÜV NORD JI / CDM
Certification Program to carry out the 5th verification of the project “Guaraí, Itabira and Santa Izabel
Ceramics Fuel Switching Project” with regard to the requirements of VCS Version 3.
The project activity involves fuel switching from heavy oil to renewable biomasses (wood from
afforestation and wood residues) to feed their kilns in order to minimize environmental impacts related to
the fossil fuel consumption. Thus, GHG emission reduction is achieved.
The project applies the CDM approved methodology AMS-I.C – Thermal energy for the user with or
without electricity – version 13.0.
In the course of the VCS Verification, 03 Correction Action Requests (CAR) and 04 Clarification Requests
(CL) were raised and successfully closed.
The VCS Verification was based on the draft monitoring report/MR/, emission reduction calculation
spreadsheet/XLS/, the monitoring plan as set out in the validated PD/VCS-PD/ and supporting documents
made available to the TÜV NORD JI/CDM CP by the project participant.
Verification Period: from 01-March-2013 to 31-March-2016 (including both days)
As a result of this verification, the verifier confirms that:
 all operations of the project are implemented and installed as planned and described in the
validated project design document;
 the monitoring plan is in accordance with the applied approved CDM methodology, i.e., AMS-I.C
– version 13.0;
 the installed equipment essential for measuring parameters required for calculating emission
reductions are properly maintained;
 the monitoring system is in place and functional. The project has generated GHG emission
reductions.
In addition, the verifier confirms that the GHG emission reductions are calculated without material
misstatements in a conservative and appropriate manner. TÜV NORD JI/CDM CP herewith confirms that
the project has achieved emission reductions in the above mentioned reporting period as follows:
Emission reductions: 64,929 t CO e

1 INTRODUCTION
Sustainable Carbon – Projetos Ambientais Ltda. has commissioned the TÜV NORD JI / CDM
Certification Program to carry out the 5th verification of the project:
“Guaraí, Itabira and Santa Izabel Ceramics Fuel Switching Project”
with regard to the relevant requirements of the Verified Carbon Standard Version 3/VCS/. The verifier
has reviewed the implementation of the monitoring plan in the VCS project for the monitoring period
2013-03-01 to 2016-03-31 (including both days).
The applied monitoring methodology is AMS-I.C – Thermal energy for the user with or without
electricity – version 13.0.
1.1 Objective
The purpose of this verification, by independent checking of objective evidence, is as follows:
 to verify that the project is implemented as described in the project design document;
 to assess the implementation of the monitoring plan content in the VCS-PD;
 to assess the project’s compliance with other relevant rules, including the host country (Brazil)
legislation;
 to confirm that the monitoring system is implemented and fully functional to generate voluntary
emission reductions (VERs / VCUs ) or ERs without any double counting; and
 to establish that the data reported are accurate, complete, consistent, transparent and free of
material error or omission by checking the monitoring records and the emissions reduction
calculation.
1.2 Scope and Criteria
The verification of this project is based on the validated project design document/VCS-PD/, the
monitoring report/MR/, emission reduction calculation spread sheet/XLS/, supporting documents made
available to the verifier and information collected through performing interviews and during the on-
site assessment. Furthermore, publicly available information was considered as far as available
and required.
The TÜV NORD JI/CDM CP has employed a risk-based approach in the verification, focusing on
the identification of significant risks and reliability of project monitoring and generation of emission
reductions.
1.3 Level of Assurance
The verification has been planned and organized to achieve a
reasonable level of assurance
limited level of assurance
1.4 Summary Description of the Project
Located in Itaboraí, state of Rio de Janeiro – Brazil, the project comprises the ceramics Guaraí,
Itabira and Santa Izabel, which produces structural ceramic devices. Guaraí works with six kilns
(one Hoffmann and five Round); Itabira works with one Hoffmann kiln and Santa Izabel works with
one Hoffmann and three Intermittent Reversible kilns The project activity involves fuel switching
from heavy oil to renewable biomasses (wood from afforestation and wood residues) to feed their
kilns. Thus, GHG emission reduction is achieved.

- verification planning;
- on-site assessment;

Furthermore, the on-site assessment is necessary to check the monitoring data with respect to
accuracy to ensure the calculation of emission reductions. The main tasks covered during the site
visit include, but are not limited to:

2.5 Resolution of Findings
Material discrepancies identified in the course of the verification are addressed either as CARs,
CLs or FARs.
A Corrective Action Request (CAR) is established where:
 mistakes have been made in assumptions, application of the methodology or the project
documentation which will have a direct influence on the project results,
 the requirements deemed relevant for verification of the project with certain characteristics have
not been met, or
 there is a risk that the project would not be registered or that emission reductions would not be
able to be verified and certified.
A Clarification Request (CL) will be issued where information is insufficient, unclear or not
transparent enough to establish whether a requirement is met.
A Forward Action Request (FAR) will be issued when certain issues related to project
implementation should be reviewed during the next verification.
A detailed list of the CARs CLs and FAR raised and discussed in the course of this verification is
included in Section 4 of this report.
2.5.1 Forward Action Requests
No FAR has been raised.

3.2 Methodology Deviations
The project activity complies with the applied methodology AMS-I.C – Thermal energy for the user
with or without electricity – version 13.0.
No deviations have been observed.
3.3 Project Description Deviations
During the verification, a site visit was carried out. Based on this site visit and the reviewed project
documentation, it can be confirmed that w.r.t. the realized technology, project equipment, as well
as the monitoring equipment.
The project has been implemented and operated as described in the registered VCS-PD, with the
correction already implemented at the last monitoring period and accepted by VCS, which is that
the registered VCS-PD sets that the project proponent (meaning the ceramic owner) would
measure the amount of renewable biomasses used (parameter Q ). However, the parameter
renbiomass
is monitored through the receipts and invoices of biomass received by the ceramic company. This
means that the amount of renewable biomass is measured by the supplier and controlled by the
ceramic owner by the receipts and invoices. This approach considers that it is the responsibility of
the provider to measure the amount of biomass, since this information needs to be available in the
sale invoice or receipt. As this information is used for commercial purposes, it is considered that
data from the suppliers are a reliable source for this parameter.
In addition, a correction was made with the type of kilns used at Ceramic Santa Izabel. At the
validated PD, the kilns were erroneously described as one Hoffman and three Round kilns. In fact,
the ceramic always used one Hoffman and three Intermittent Reversible kilns. The correction has
no impact in the applicability of the methodology, additionality of the project activity or to the
appropriateness of the baseline scenario.
3.4 Grouped Project
Not applicable as the proposed project activity is not part of grouped projects.

4.1.1 Implementation status of the project activity
Description
The project started the fuel switch on 2003-01-01 to Ceramic Guaraí, on 2004-10-01 to Ceramic
Itabira and 2004-03-01 to Ceramic Santa Izabel.
Project activity was operational during the current monitoring period. Monitoring records were
checked.
Ceramic Guaraí works with six kilns (one Hoffmann and five Round); Ceramic Itabira works with
one Hoffmann kiln. Those remained exactly as described at the validated PD.
Ceramic Santa Izabel works with one Hoffmann and three Intermittent Reversible kilns. This is the
original configuration of the ceramic, which has been erroneously described at the validated PD.
This correction has no impact in the applicability of the methodology, additionality of the project
activity or to the appropriateness of the baseline scenario.
Some improvements as solar dryers, new machines and trucks have been implemented at the
ceramics in order to have better commercial performance and better working conditions.
As per the on-site assessment, observations, interviews and collected evidences, it was possible
to assess that, in general, the project activity has been implemented as described in the Project
Design/VCS-PD/ with the changes already accepted by VCS at the last verification and that are
described above at Section 3.3 of this report.
The crediting period is from 2006-04-01 to 2016-03-31 (both days included) and renewable for two
more periods of 10 years.

4.2 Accuracy of GHG Emission Reduction and Removal Calculations
Description
- The approved CDM methodology AMS-I.C – Thermal energy for the user with or without
electricity – version 13.0 is applied to the project activity. GHG emission reduction is calculated
as baseline emissions.
- No project emissions are considered by the applied methodology.
- No leakage is considered for this kind of project as all three possible sources of leakage are
demonstrated at the MR as:
o there is no transference of equipment;
o the amount of wood from afforestation consumed by the project activity represents less than
1% of their availability within the surrounding areas, which avoids the possibility of leakage;
o the amount of wood residues consumed by the project activity represents less than 1% of
their availability within the surrounding areas, which avoids the possibility of leakage.
Thus, the leakage is not considered in accordance with the applied “General guidance on
leakage in biomass project activities”, as the source of leakage can be neglected if it is
demonstrated at the beginning of each crediting period that the quantity of available biomass in
the region is at least 25% larger than the quantity of biomass that is utilized, including the project
activity.
- All formulas used in the calculations of emission reductions are in accordance to the approved
methodology AMS-I.C/AMS/ and monitoring plan of the validated PD/VCS-PD/.
- All records provided were found to be of adequate quality. The verification team has
crosschecked the resulted value of units supplied by the project activity with the sources and it
is found to be correct.
- The set of calculations for the actual emission reduction of the current verification period are
transparently computed and presented in the spreadsheet of ER calculation/XLS/.
Related Findings
No CARs, CLs or FARs have been identified in this context

6 VERIFICATION CONCLUSION
Sustainable Carbon – Projetos Ambientais Ltda. has commissioned the TÜV NORD JI/CDM
Certification Program to carry out the 5th periodic verification of the project “Guaraí, Itabira and
Santa Izabel Ceramics Fuel Switching Project”, with regard to the relevant requirements of VCS
Version 3.
The project activity involves fuel switching from heavy oil to renewable biomasses (wood from
afforestation and wood residues) to feed their kilns. Thus, GHG emission reduction is achieved.
The verification is based on the draft monitoring report/MR/, emission reduction calculation
spreadsheet/XLS/, the monitoring plan as set out in the validated PD/VCS-PD/ with changes accepted
at the last verification/VER/ and supporting documents made available to the TÜV NORD JI/CDM CP
by the project participants.
In detail, the conclusions can be summarized as follows:
- all operations of the project are implemented and installed as planned and described in the
validated project description and project activity confirms with the verification criteria for project
and their GHG emission reductions or removals set out in the VCS rules;
- the monitoring plan is in accordance with the applied approved methodology, i.e. AMS-I.C –
version 13.0;
- the installed equipment essential for monitoring parameters required for calculating emission
reductions are appropriate;
- the monitoring system is in place and functional. The project has generated verifiable GHG
emission reductions.
As the result of the verification, the verifier confirms that the GHG emission reductions are
calculated without material misstatements in a conservative and appropriate manner. TÜV NORD
JI/CDM CP herewith confirms that the project has achieved emission reductions as presented
below.

A Sustainable Carbon é especialista e líder na América Latina no desenvolvimento
de projetos de reduções de emissões e de soluções ligadas ao gerenciamento de
GasesdeEfeitoEstufa,GEEs.
Nossas soluções permitem que organizações e indivíduos conheçam e reduzam
seus impactos ambientais, possibilitando o equilíbrio das emissões e contribuindo
paraodesenvolvimentosustentáveldecomunidadesnomundotodo.
Também conectamos pessoas querendo fazer a diferença no mundo: empresários,
desenvolvedoresdeprojetos,empresas,ONGs,governos,associaçõeseentusiastas
detodoomundo.
Fazemos isso por meio de mecanismos técnicos e inovadores aprovados pelo
mercado de carbono que quantificam as reduções das emissões GEE.
Estabelecendo, assim, um preço sobre esse serviço ambiental e permitindo o
comérciointernacionaldestacommodityúnica:ocréditodecarbono.
Assim, aqueles que reduzem suas emissões recebem por isso e são capazes de
manter os projetos de carbono, que preservam ecossistemas ameaçados e geram
desenvolvimentonaregiãoondeestãolocalizados.
Somos especialistas no desenvolvimento de projetos de energia renovável, de
substituição de combustível, compostagem, projetos de biomassa e projetos
florestais.
Realizamos trabalhos em quase todos os ecossistemas brasileiros. Foi assim que

Informações institucionais
A Sustainable Carbon é especialista e líder na América Latina no desenvolvimento de
projetos de reduções de emissões e de soluções ligadas ao gerenciamento de Gases de
Efeito Estufa, GEEs.
Nossas soluções permitem que organizações e indivíduos conheçam e reduzam seus
impactos ambientais, possibilitando o equilíbrio das emissões e contribuindo para o
desenvolvimento sustentável de comunidades no mundo todo.
Também conectamos pessoas querendo fazer a diferença no mundo: empresários,
desenvolvedores de projetos, empresas, ONGs, governos, associações e entusiastas de
todo o mundo.
Fazemos isso por meio de mecanismos técnicos e inovadores aprovados pelo mercado de
carbono que quantificam as reduções das emissões GEE. Estabelecendo, assim, um preço
sobre esse serviço ambiental e permitindo o comércio internacional desta commodity
única: o crédito de carbono.
Assim, aqueles que reduzem suas emissões recebem por isso e são capazes de manter os
projetos de carbono, que preservam ecossistemas ameaçados e geram desenvolvimento
na região onde estão localizados.
Somos especialistas no desenvolvimento de projetos de energia renovável, de
substituição de combustível, compostagem, projetos de biomassa e projetos florestais.
Realizamos trabalhos em quase todos os ecossistemas brasileiros. Foi assim que nos
últimos anos a Sustainable Carbon entregou créditos de carbono da mais alta qualidade
para compensações e hoje é a empresa líder neste segmento na América Latina.
Veja abaixo nossas conquistas:
Somos a empresa líder mundial em desenvolvimento de projetos para o mercado
voluntário (VER), gerenciando um portfólio com mais de 50 projetos validados e mais de
16M de toneladas em reduções de emissões;
Pioneira no desenvolvimento de melhores práticas sustentáveis, validamos o primeiro
projeto SOCIALCARBON® em 2008 e o primeiro projeto Gold Standard para o mercado
voluntário no Brasil em 2011;
Mais de 50 comunidades já foram beneficiadas através dos créditos de carbono gerados
para o desenvolvimento sustentável;
Soluções de GEEs para mais de 100 empresas em 23 países.

4.1 Informações sobre a performance da organização, em comparação com benchmarks internos (ex: outras
unidades) ou externos (ex: organizações do mesmo setor).
As emissões relatadas no Inventário da Sustainable Carbon para este ano apresentaram um aumento referente ao
consumo de energia elétrica, o que pode ser explicado pelo aumento no número de funcionários em relação à
2018. No escopo 3, a principal fonte de emissões da empresa é "Viagens a negócios", assim como em 2018, porém
neste ano houve uma redução de emissões, que pode ser explicado pelo menor número de viagens realizadas em
2019.

2
fabricados.
Não foi relatado.
4.3 Descrição de estratégias e projetos para a gestão de emissões de GEE.
Uma vez que grande parte das emissões estão alocadas no escopo 3, a Sustainable Carbon trabalha com algumas
medidas e princípios para diminuir as emissões ao longo do tempo. É prática comum da empresa: realizar reuniões
via vídeo conferência, evitando assim o deslocamento de ida e volta até o local da reunião. Quando realiza visita de
verificação dos projetos, opta por fazer a visita de um polo inteiro, passando por todos os projetos do Estado de
uma única vez.
4.4 Informações sobre contratos com clientes e fornecedores que incluam cláusulas vinculadas à elaboração de
inventários de GEE e/ou ao envio de informações relacionadas.
Não foi relatado.
4.5 Informações sobre incertezas, exclusões de fontes de dados e outras características da elaboração do
inventário.
A administração do condomínio é responsável pelo controle da energia elétrica adquirida para a área comum do
prédio, essas emissões foram excluídas do inventário de 2019, por dificuldade na coleta de dados e por serem
irrelevantes.
4.6 Descrição sobre ações internas para melhoria da qualidade do inventário de GEE. Por exemplo, sistematização
da coleta de dados, contratação de verificação externa, etc.
A Sustainable Carbon desenvolve sistematização de coleta de dados que abrange todo o escopo de suas atividades
organizacionais, através de ferramentas corporativas e a difusão interna do conhecimento sobre as emissões de
GEE. Para a coleta interna dos dados, criamos uma plataforma com acesso a todos os colaboradores. Este
instrumento possibilita a coleta dos dados assim que ocorre a atividade e sua emissão, dessa forma facilita o
monitoramento dos indicadores, realização do inventário e também o processo de verificação por terceira parte.
Assim como os instrumentos corporativos, o conhecimento compartilhado junto aos colaboradores mostra-se
importante para a qualidade e transparência do inventário. Todos os colaboradores passam por orientações quanto

Summary:
The project activity encompasses Lara Ceramic which is a ceramic industry factory located in
Cachoeira Paulista, in the state of São Paulo, Brazil. The ceramic factory produces structural ceramic
v3.3 1
VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
devices like bricks, destined for the local market of Vale do Paraíba region. The ceramic pieces
produced in Lara Ceramic obey the ABNT1 norms. The fuel utilized to produce the ceramic devices in
the baseline scenario was heavy oil.
The objective of the implementation of this project activity in the ceramic factory is to stop using the
heavy oil and completely substitute it with renewable biomasses to feed their kilns and cook the
ceramic pieces so that they can minimize environmental impacts related to the fossil fuel consumption.
According to the VCS PD, the project activity may use as renewable biomasses: sawdust, wood chips,
non-fossil fraction of industrial or municipal waste, rice husk, elephant grass, and afforestation wood.
There were major non conformities found during the onsite assessment such as the fact that were no
clear records of the production of ceramic pieces. After the CARs raised the project owners
reorganized their files and all of the hard copies of the production notes were handed over. Therefore
closing all the non-conformities properly.
The Monitoring Report is considered to be in compliance with VCS Program Guide, the approved
methodology AMS I.C (version 13) and the Monitoring Plan presented in the Project Description.
During the monitoring period from 01/07/2008 to 28/02/2014 the project generated the following GHG
emission reductions: 46,473 tCO e.
2
IBOPE does not take any responsibility towards the issuance and utilization of the emission reductions
hereby verified and certified. Request for issuance of VCUs shall be made by the project proponent to
an approved VCS Program Registry based on the requirements set out under the most recent version
of the VCS Program Guidelines clause on VCS Registration. The verification of reported emission
reductions is based on the information made available to us and the engagement conditions detailed in
this report. IBOPE cannot guarantee the accuracy or correctness of this information.

VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
1 INTRODUCTION
1.1 Objective
Sustainable Carbon – Projetos Ambientais LTDA has commissioned IBOPE to verify the 2nd periodic
verification of the emission reductions reported for the VCS “Lara Ceramic Fuel Switching Project” with
regard to the relevant requirements for VCS program as per the Verified Carbon Standard Version 3.
The objective of the the 2nd periodic verification is to verify that the actual monitoring systems and
procedures are in compliance with the monitoring system and procedures described in the Monitoring
Plan that is presented in the PD as well as with the Monitoring Manual. In addition, the periodic
verification evaluates the greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions data presented in the Monitoring Report and
expresses a conclusion about whether the presented GHG emissions data is free off material
misstatements and sufficiently supported by evidence.
1.2 Scope and Criteria
The verification comprises a review of the monitoring report over the monitoring period from 01-July-2008
to 28-February-2014, based on the registered Project Description (PD) in part of the project design,
monitoring parameters, monitoring plan, emission reduction calculation, monitoring methodology and all
related evidences provided by project participant. Those documents have been reviewed and verified
against VCS Version 3 requirements, UNFCCC rules and associated interpretations. The verification is
based on validated project design document including baseline calculations.
The main scopes of this verification are:
 Verify whether the emission reductions generated by the project are in line with the VCS
Verification Protocol and the information provided by the project participants contains all the
necessary information to evidence the project’s compliance with all criteria in the VCS.
 Verify that the project has been implemented as described in the Project Design (PD) during the
verification period.
 Confirm that the monitoring plan was implemented and fully functional to generate voluntary
emission reductions (VCU) without any double counting during the whole verification period.
 Give a conclusion whether reported data are accurate, complete, consistent, and transparent,
with a high level of assurance and free of material error or misstatement.
In particular, the project baseline, monitoring plan and the project compliance with relevant applicable
protocols and criteria (i.e. UNFCCC, VCS, host Party and others) are to be validated in order to confirm
that the project design, as documented, is sound and reasonable and meets the applicable criteria.
v3.3 4
VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
The correct application of the CDM small scale methodology: “AMS-I.C.: Thermal energy for the user with
or without electricity – Version 13” was already validated by TÜV Nord Cert GmbH, documented in its
Validation Report of November 6th 2009.
An explicit review of the validation and its underlying assumptions made and conclusion of TÜV Nord Cert
GmbH are not scope of this verification.
1.3 Level of Assurance
As the VCS Version 3 recognizes verified emission reductions, IBOPE has focused on providing a
reasonable level of assurance that the emission reduction calculation methodology is appropriate and
correctly applied, and that emission reductions have been accurately monitored. The implementation can
be considered sustainable and no indications were observed leading to the assumption of high risks
beyond common project specific challenges in relation to the likelihood of continual removal of GHG
emission reduction through the project. This judgment is based on the document review, on site checks
and consistency checks.
In the case it is required, IBOPE may discount verified emission reductions or requests a discount of
these by using conservative assumptions for uncertainties in emission reduction calculations that cannot
be fully quantified or that cannot give a desired level of assurance. For verifying/certifying VCUs, the
desired level of assurance was based on the combined quantitative assessment of the accuracy of
monitoring project performance and the identification of material risks.
1.4 Summary Description of the Project
The project activity encompasses Lara Ceramic which is a ceramic industry factory located in Cachoeira
Paulista, in the state of São Paulo, Brazil. The ceramic factory produces structural ceramic devices like
bricks, destined for the local market of Vale do Paraíba region. The ceramic pieces produced in Lara
Ceramic obey the ABNT2 norms. The fuel utilized to produce the ceramic devices in the baseline
scenario was heavy oil.
The objective of the implementation of this project activity in the ceramic factory is to stop using the heavy
oil and completely substitute it with renewable biomasses to feed their kilns and cook the ceramic pieces
so that they can minimize environmental impacts related to the fossil fuel consumption.
According to the VCS PD, the project activity may use as renewable biomasses: sawdust, wood chips,
non-fossil fraction of industrial or municipal waste, rice husk, elephant grass, and afforestation wood.
2Brazilian Association of Technical Standards (ABNT) is the body responsible for technical standardization in Brazil,
providing the necessary basis for the Brazilian technological development.
v3.3 5
VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
Therefore, this fuel switching project reduces the greenhouse gases emissions through substitution of
heavy oil for biomass for thermal energy generation. This fuel switch could only be feasible when
considering the carbon credits incomes, since the adaptation of machineries to the new biomasses and
the purchase of new equipments required considerable investments. The ceramic sector is very resistant
to changes and improvements in its work process. The modifications required for the fuel switch are an
innovation in the region and represent a first step in the sector to revert this situation. This way, it also
stimulates regional development.
The main difficulties for the Ceramic factory due to the fuel-switch were the non-availability of human
knowledge to operate and maintain the new technology implemented, as well as the internal logistic
modification and the employee’s resistance to the new technology.
The Ceramic lost production due to the adaptation to the use of biomasses and to the new machineries,
as they added significant differences to the production process. Furthermore, during the kilns’ reform
period, the ceramic company faced some problems and consequently had lost their production.
Besides, these new equipments acquired as well as the adaptation in the kilns added an additional cost
for the ceramics.
The operators did not have knowledge of the ideal amount of renewable biomasses that was necessary
to achieve the ideal temperature for the ceramic devices cooking in order to acquire the final product with
same quality and to maintain the optimal process as they did when using heavy oil. Therefore, some
training courses were required for the staffs of the ceramic industry in order to clarify new measures
linked to the machinery, sustaining the quality of the final product and find a standard burn cycle.
Moreover, although there is currently a great amount of these types of biomasses available regionally, a
demand and price increase has already been reported by the project proponent. Even if the price of the
biomasses increase, the ceramic factory cannot repass it through the sale of ceramic pieces, once they
would not have competitive prices in relation to other ceramics which did not make the fuel switch. The
project approval will make the continuous use of renewable biomasses feasible.
By diverging significantly from the identified baseline scenario, with the implementation of this project, the
ceramic factory now generate thermal energy minimizing the damages caused by the use of fossil fuels
and preserving oil reserves. Furthermore, the ceramic dispense the oil transportation, which could result
in huge environmental impacts.
v3.3 6
VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
2 VERIFICATION PROCESS
2.1 Method and Criteria
The verification is based on validated project description including baseline and monitoring report. These
documents are reviewed against VCS Version 3 requirements. IBOPE has, based on the
recommendations in the Validation and Verification Standard, employed a risk-based approach in the
verification, focusing on the identification of significant risks and reliability of project monitoring and
generation of emission reductions.
The verification includes the next steps: desk review of the project design documents and monitoring
report; on-site visit and follow-up interviews and resolution of outstanding issues and the issuance of the
final verification report and opinion.
2.2 Document Review
The following table outlines the documentation reviewed during the verification process:
/ 1/ UNFCCC, Clean Development Mechanism Validation and Verification Standard, Version 02.0
/ 2/ UNFCCC, CDM small scale methodology “AMS-I.C: Thermal energy for the user with or without
electricity” – Version 13.
/ 3/ VCS PD registered, Version 5.1 – November 3rd, 2009
/ 4/ VCS Validation Report - TÜV NORD CERT GmbH - November 6th, 2009
/ 5/ VCS Verification Report - TÜV NORD CERT GmbH - November 6th, 2009
/8/ VCS Monitoring Report - First Draft - Version 01.1 - April 02nd, 2014
/ 9/ VCS Monitoring Report - Last version - Version 02 - April 16th, 2014
/ 10/ Emission Reductions Calculation - Spreadsheet “VCS Calculation MR Lara_period_01_01 07 08_
28 02 14 v.1.xls”
/ 11/ Emission Reductions Calculation - Spreadsheet “VCS Calculation MR Lara_period_01_01 07 08_
28 02 14 v.2.xls”
2.3 Interviews
IBOPE has performed an on-site visit on April 10th to Lara Ceramic during which the operations of the
ceramic were reviewed. During the visit the verification team conducted interviews with the following
representatives of the project company Lara Ceramic and the Sustainable Carbon – Projetos Ambientais
Ltda who prepared the VCS PD and the VCS Monitoring Report:
 Mr. Eduardo Lara, Director and owner of Lara Ceramic
 Mr. Átila Bernardes Nunes, Manager, monitoring data responsible of Lara Ceramic
v3.3 7
VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
 Mrs. Beatriz Rovere, of Sustainable Carbon – Projetos Ambientais Ltda.
The monitoring report was completed by Marcelo Hector Sabbagh Haddad, Beatriz Rovere, Larissa Tega
da Fonseca and Denis Gonçalves dos Santos, from Sustainable Carbon – Projetos Ambientais LTDA.
2.4 Site Inspections
An on-site inspection was conducted on 10/04/2014 with the purpose to clarify whether the project had
been carried out as described in the VCS PD, assessment of documents and interview people in order to
verify the project conditions. The main verification topics during the site visit include, but are not limited to:
- On-site assessment to confirm whether all relevant equipment are installed and works as described in
the registered VCS PD;
- The operating staff was interviewed and observed in order to check the risks of inappropriate operation
and data collection procedures;
- Information process for generating, aggregating and reporting the selected monitored parameters were
reviewed;
- The monitoring processes, routines and documentations were audited to check their proper application;
- The monitoring data were checked completely.
The on-site visit at the ceramic confirmed that the monitoring and reporting is carried out consistently and
in line with established procedures and as per the requirements of the monitoring plan mentioned in the
registered VCS PD.
2.5 Resolution of Findings
This process is aimed at resolving any outstanding issues which need to be clarified prior to the IBOPE
conclusion on the project design and monitoring results. In order to ensure transparency a verification
protocol is customized for the project. The protocol shows in transparent manner criteria (requirements),
means of verification and their results against the identified criteria.
Findings established during the verification can either be seen as a non-fulfillment of CDM criteria and
VCS Version 3, or where a risk to the fulfillment of project objectives is identified. Corrective action
requests (CAR) issued where mistakes have been made with a direct influence on project results.
Moreover, clarifications (CL) were requested where additional information is needed to fully clarify an
issue.
v3.3 8
VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
During the course of this Verification, it was checked that all of the data from the spreadsheet was backed
up by solid proof via receipts in the case of purchases of burning material or process logs for the
production of ceramic pieces. The calculations of emission reductions were revised and proofed. In any of
the cases, there were no discrepancies or mistakes found.
This verification came out with 6 (six) corrective action request, which were all listed in Appendix A. They
have all been attended and closed.

3 VALIDATION FINDINGS
Not applicable.
3.1 Participation under Other GHG Programs
Not applicable.
3.2 Methodology Deviations
There were no methodology deviations during this monitoring period.
3.3 Project Description Deviations
The registered VCS PD version 5.1 establishes that the project proponent (meaning the ceramic
owner) would measure the amount of renewable biomasses used (parameter Q ).
renbiomass
However, during the entire monitored period, this parameter was monitored through all the
receipts and invoices of biomass received by the ceramic industry. This means the amount of
renewable biomass is measured by each provider and controlled by the ceramic owner, by
storing receipts and invoices.
This approach was chosen considering that it is the responsibility of the provider to measure the
amount of biomass, since this information needs to be available in the sale invoice or receipt. As
this information is used for commercial purposes (to calculate due financial compensations), it is
considered that data from the suppliers are a reliable source for this parameter..
3.4 Grouped Project
Not applicable.
v3.3 10
VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
4 VERIFICATION FINDINGS
4.1 Project Implementation Status
The project consists in fuel switching, substitution of native wood for renewable biomasses to generate
thermal energy, feeding the kilns and fire the ceramic units from the Lara Ceramic, according to the VCS
PD.
During the on-site visit, it was physically observed by the verification team that renewable biomasses
such as sawdust and wood waste like staple have been employed on the kilns for energy supply.
The project implementation status has follows:
Start date January 1st 2004
First crediting period (10 years, twice renewable) April 1st, 2006 to March 31th ,2016
Total Emission Reductions estimated for the
period: 102,750 tCO2e
1st Periodic Verification April 1st, 2006 to June 30th ,2008
Emission Reductions: 19,124 tCO2e
2nd Periodic Verification July 1st, 2008 to February 28th, 2014.
Emission Reductions: 46,473 tCO2e
There are no remaining issues from previous validation and verifications. The six (06) CARs raised during
this verification process were satisfactorily answered by the project proponent and are closed.
The monitored period for this verification process comprises from 01 July 2008 to 28 February 2014. The
production of the monitored period was 167,284 tonnes of ceramic units. The verification team checked
the internal control sheet that contains the number of ceramic pieces produced that comes into each of
the kilns. There was no big difference with the production since the only Hoffmann kiln wasn’t replaced or
a new one added.
The additionality assessment was validated based on assessing the comparison made between the cost
of energy generated on the baseline scenario of Lara Ceramic Fuel Switching Project and at the current
monitoring period, detailed in Table 01 of VCS MR. The verification team was able to verify that all
assumptions have been used in a proper manner, thus it is consistent to affirm that the project is still
additional.
According to the VCS PD, in order to the project is in accordance with local laws, it must run with licenses
under valid time. During the on-site visit, the verification team checked the environmental license and clay
v3.3 11
VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
extraction license and they were valid during this monitored period, confirming the project compliance
with the local laws.
The registered VCS PD version 5.1 establishes that the project proponent (meaning the ceramic owner)
would measure the amount of renewable biomasses used (parameter Qrenbiomass). However, during
the entire monitored period, this parameter was monitored through all the receipts and invoices of
biomass received by the ceramic industry. This means the amount of renewable biomass is
measured by each provider and controlled by the ceramic owner, by storing receipts and invoices.
This approach was chosen considering that it is the responsibility of the provider to measure the amount
of biomass, since this information needs to be available in the sale invoice or receipt. As this
information is used for commercial purposes (to calculate due financial compensations), it is considered
that data from the suppliers are a reliable source for this parameter.
4.2 Accuracy of GHG Emission Reduction and Removal Calculations
The verification team has checked the emission reduction calculation and confirmed that the Baseline
Emissions have been calculated according to the formulas in the PD and following the approved CDM
baseline and monitoring methodology “AMS-I.C: Thermal energy for the user with or without electricity” –
Version 13.
The methodology applied is Category AMS-I.C.: Thermal energy for the user with or without electricity –
Version 13 valid from 28-March-2008 to 07-April-2009, which is applicable for project activities that avoid
greenhouse gases emissions by using renewable biomass instead of fossil fuels, in order to generate
thermal energy.
The project activity in the current monitoring period (68 months) generated 91.65 TJ per year. Converting
this number to MWh it was generated around 25,459 MWh per year, which corresponds to the use of 2.91
MWthermal, which is less than the limit of 45 MWthermal for Type I small scale project activities.
The calculations for the baseline were realized as established in the following equations:

4.3 Quality of Evidence to Determine GHG Emission Reductions and Removals
IBOPE was able to verify that Lara Ceramic Fuel Switching Project has adequate monitoring mechanisms
where the required parameters have been monitored on a monthly basis. This was verified during the on-
site visit when the plant records were verified to assess the correctness of the reported data.
The amount of renewable biomass employed was checked by accessing all the receipts and invoices of
biomass received by the ceramic. It was all found in order.
The production of ceramic units per month was checked by accessing the internal control sheets used by
Lara Ceramic, which are organized per month and daily filled according to data based on ceramic units
v3.3 13
VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
burned in the kilns. At the beginning there were no hard copies shown to the verification team, hence six
corrective action requests were raised. This has been corrected by showing all the hard copies.
The Emission Reductions have been correctly calculated. A complete set of data for the specified
monitoring period was available and verified while undertaking site visit plant records maintained at site
(after raising the CARs). As no further uncertainties or quality restriction affect the calculations the
verification team has reviewed the above-mentioned evidences and considered that calculations are in
line with VCS v.3 requirements.
4.4 Non-Permanence Risk Analysis
IBOPE conducted a risk analysis during the Contract Review phase, before finish the Verification
process and considers the risk rating acceptable.
v3.3 14
VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
5 VERIFICATION CONCLUSION
The verification team of IBOPE has performed the 2nd Verification for the Lara Ceramic Fuel Switching
Project in Brazil on the basis of VCS Version 3 and UNFCCC criteria for the CDM, as well as criteria
given to provide for consistent project operations, monitoring and reporting.
IBOPE concludes that during the period of July 1st 2008 to February 28th 2014 the above described
project has fulfilled all the requirements to produce emissions reductions on the total of 46,473 tCO2 eq.
The project is implemented as planned and described in the registered VCS Project Design. The
monitoring plan is in place and the project is generating GHG emission reductions. IBOPE concludes that
the project is implemented and operated as planned and described in the registered VCS Project Design;
The monitoring system is in place and functional. The project has generated GHG emission reductions.
It is the opinion of IBOPE that this verification job to be considered as closed.

Informações institucionais
A Sustainable Carbon é especialista e líder na América Latina no desenvolvimento de
projetos de reduções de emissões e de soluções ligadas ao gerenciamento de Gases de
Efeito Estufa, GEEs.
Nossas soluções permitem que organizações e indivíduos conheçam e reduzam seus
impactos ambientais, possibilitando o equilíbrio das emissões e contribuindo para o
desenvolvimento sustentável de comunidades no mundo todo.
Também conectamos pessoas querendo fazer a diferença no mundo: empresários,
desenvolvedores de projetos, empresas, ONGs, governos, associações e entusiastas de
todo o mundo.
Fazemos isso por meio de mecanismos técnicos e inovadores aprovados pelo mercado de
carbono que quantificam as reduções das emissões GEE. Estabelecendo, assim, um preço
sobre esse serviço ambiental e permitindo o comércio internacional desta commodity
única: o crédito de carbono.
Assim, aqueles que reduzem suas emissões recebem por isso e são capazes de manter os
projetos de carbono, que preservam ecossistemas ameaçados e geram desenvolvimento
na região onde estão localizados.
Somos especialistas no desenvolvimento de projetos de energia renovável, de
substituição de combustível, compostagem, projetos de biomassa e projetos florestais.
Realizamos trabalhos em quase todos os ecossistemas brasileiros. Foi assim que nos
últimos anos a Sustainable Carbon entregou créditos de carbono da mais alta qualidade
para compensações e hoje é a empresa líder neste segmento na América Latina.
Veja abaixo nossas conquistas:
Somos a empresa líder mundial em desenvolvimento de projetos para o mercado
voluntário (VER), gerenciando um portfólio com mais de 50 projetos validados e mais de
16M de toneladas em reduções de emissões;
Pioneira no desenvolvimento de melhores práticas sustentáveis, validamos o primeiro
projeto SOCIALCARBON® em 2008 e o primeiro projeto Gold Standard para o mercado
voluntário no Brasil em 2011;
Mais de 50 comunidades já foram beneficiadas através dos créditos de carbono gerados
para o desenvolvimento sustentável;
Soluções de GEEs para mais de 100 empresas em 23 países.

4. Outros Elementos
Campos de preenchimento opcional
4.1 Informações sobre a performance da organização, em comparação com benchmarks internos (ex: outras
unidades) ou externos (ex: organizações do mesmo setor).
As emissões relatadas no Inventário da Sustainable Carbon para este ano apresentaram reduções consideráveis
nos escopos 2 e 3. Isso se deve principalmente à pandemia de Covid-19, desde março de 2020, que fez com que
todas as atividades da empresa fossem realizadas em sistema de teletrabalho. Dessa forma, houve menor consumo
de energia elétrica no escritório e menos viagens de deslocamento casa-trabalho dos funcionários. "Viagens a
Negócios" ainda corresponde à maior fonte de emissões do escopo 3, porém, sofreu uma redução de mais de 20
tCO2e em comparação ao último inventário. Para o ano de 2020, também foram reportadas emissões de energia
elétrica no escopo 3, correspondentes ao consumo dos funcionários durante o expediente de trabalho em casa,
tendo como referência o consumo no escritório durante o ano de 2019.
4.2 Descrição de indicadores de emissão de GEE para as atividades da organização. Por exemplo, tCO e/produtos
2
fabricados.
Não foi relatado.
4.3 Descrição de estratégias e projetos para a gestão de emissões de GEE.
Uma vez que grande parte das emissões estão alocadas no escopo 3, a Sustainable Carbon trabalha com algumas
medidas e princípios para diminuir as emissões ao longo do tempo. É prática comum da empresa: realizar reuniões
via vídeo conferência, evitando assim o deslocamento de ida e volta até o local da reunião. Quando realiza visita de
verificação dos projetos, opta por fazer a visita de um polo inteiro, passando por todos os projetos do Estado de
uma única vez. Durante o ano de 2020, essas ações tiveram ainda mais importância, visando reduzir a exposição
dos funcionários à doenças, e, por consequência, resultaram na redução de emissões em todos os escopos
reportados.
4.4 Informações sobre contratos com clientes e fornecedores que incluam cláusulas vinculadas à elaboração de

irrelevantes. Para o ano de 2020, também foram reportadas emissões de energia elétrica no escopo 3,
correspondentes ao consumo dos funcionários durante o expediente de trabalho em casa, tendo como referência o
consumo no escritório durante o ano de 2019.

da coleta de dados, contratação de verificação externa, etc.

A Sustainable Carbon desenvolve sistematização de coleta de dados que abrange todo o escopo de suas atividades
organizacionais, através de ferramentas corporativas e a difusão interna do conhecimento sobre as emissões de
GEE. Para a coleta interna dos dados, criamos uma plataforma com acesso a todos os colaboradores. Este
instrumento possibilita a coleta dos dados assim que ocorre a atividade e sua emissão, facilitando o monitoramento
dos indicadores, realização do inventário e também o processo de verificação por terceira parte.
Assim como os instrumentos corporativos, o conhecimento compartilhado junto aos colaboradores mostra-se
importante para a qualidade e transparência do inventário. Todos os colaboradores passam por orientações quanto
ao fornecimento dos dados necessários à elaboração do inventário, assim como a realização de treinamentos
internos, visando à difusão de conhecimento sobre os inventários de GEE.

Sol Nascente Ceramic Fuel Switching Project
Client: Project Title:
Carbono Social Serviços Ambientais Ltda. Sol Nascente Ceramic Fuel Switching Project
Summary:
Carbono Social Serviços Ambientais Ltda. has commissioned the TÜV NORD JI/CDM Certification Program to carry out the 1st
periodic verification of the project: “Sol Nascente Ceramic Fuel Switching Project”, with regard to the relevant
requirements for CDM project activities. The project reduces GHG emissions due to switching non-renewable to renewable
biomass fuel for end-user thermal energy generation This verification covers the period from 2006-04-01 to 2008-09-30
(including both days).
In the course of the verification 8 Corrective Action Requests (CAR) and 3 Clarification Request (CL) were raised and
successfully closed. Furthermore 0 FARs are raised to improve the monitoring system in the future. The verification is based
on the draft monitoring report, revised monitoring report, the monitoring plan as set out in the VCS PD, the validation report,
emission reduction calculation spreadsheet and supporting documents made available to the TÜV NORD JI/CDM CP by the
project participant.
As a result of this verification, the verifier confirms that:
• all operations of the project are implemented and installed as planned and described in the validated project design
document.
• the monitoring plan is in accordance with the applied approved CDM methodology, AMS-I.E: Switch from non-
renewable biomass for thermal applications by the user - Version 01, February 1st 2008 onwards
• the installed equipment essential for measuring parameters required for calculating emission reductions are
calibrated appropriately.
• the monitoring system is in place and functional. The project has generated GHG emission reductions.
As the result of the 1st periodic verification, the verifier confirms that the GHG emission reductions are calculated without
material misstatements in a conservative and appropriate manner. TÜV NORD JI/CDM CP herewith confirms that the project
has achieved emission reductions in the above mentioned reporting period as follows:

VCS 2007.1 Verification Report
1 Introduction
1.1 Objective
The purpose of this verification, by independent checking of objective evidence, is as
follows:
(cid:1) to verify that the project is implemented as described in the project design
document;
(cid:1) to confirm that the monitoring system is implemented and fully functional to generate
voluntary carbon units (VCUs) without any double counting, and
(cid:1) to establish that the data reported are accurate, complete, consistent, transparent
and free of material error or omission by checking the monitoring records and the
emissions reduction calculation.
1.2 Scope and Criteria
The verification of this VCS project is based on the VCS project description/VCS PD/, the
monitoring report/MR/, supporting documents made available to the verifier and
information collected through performing interviews and during the on-site assessment.
Furthermore publicly available information was considered as far as available and
required.
The TÜV NORD JI/CDM CP has employed a risk-based approach in the verification,
focusing on the identification of significant risks and reliability of project monitoring and
generation of emission reductions.
1.3 VCS project Description
Located in Panorama, Sao Paulo State, this project comprises one enterprise, Sol
Nascente Ceramic, which produces structural ceramic devices like bricks. The project
activity involves fuel switching from native firewood from Cerrado biome to sawdust and
sugar cane bagasse used to feed the ceramic kilns; thus GHG emission reduction is
achieved. Other types of renewable sources of biomass can be applied during the
project activity, such as forest residues (wood chips), elephant grass provided from
management areas and peanut shells. Before the switching of the fuel, Sol Nascente
Ceramic consumed approximately 484 tonnes of native firewood per month producing
572,000 ceramic devices per month (1,409,980 kg of pieces per month). The Ceramic
used three “Round” kilns until 2007 to produce 572,000 devices. The ceramic owner
transformed one “Round” kiln in a “Paulista/Vagão” kiln and built another one
“Paulista/Vagão” kiln which resulted in the operation of four kilns since the beginning of
2008 with the configuration of two “Round” kilns and two “Paulista/Vagão” kilns.
3
VCS 2007.1 Verification Report
The ceramic started its activities with the renewable biomass in April 2006. The Sol
Nascente Ceramic is now consuming 140 tonnes of sawdust and 90 tonnes of sugar
cane bagasse per month to produce 760,000 (1,873,400 kg of pieces per month)
devices per month. This would result in a baseline consumption of 643,72 tons of native
wood.
The GHG emission reduction for the considered crediting monitored period is
20,794tCO2e/XCS3/.
1.4 Level of assurance
The verification report is based on VCS PD, Monitoring Report, supporting documents
made available to the verifier and information collected through performing interviews
and during the on-site assessment. The verification opinion is assured provided the
credibility of all above.
2 Methodology
The verification of the project was carried out from November 2008 to August 2009.
Preparations: 2008-10-06 to 2008-10-07
On-site visit: 2008-10-08
Draft Reporting: 2009-05-29
Final Reporting: 2009-08-12
The verification consisted of the following steps:
(cid:1) A desk review of the VCS PD/VCS PD/ and supporting documents with the use of the
relevant sections of a customised protocol according to the VCS 2007.1;
(cid:1) A desk review of the Monitoring Report/MR/ and additional supporting documents
which the client submitted. The relevant sections of the above-mentioned customised
protocol according to the VCS 2007.1 were used.
(cid:1) Verification audit planning,
(cid:1) On-Site assessment,
(cid:1) Background investigation and follow-up interviews with personnel of the project
developer,
(cid:1) Verification reporting (Draft Verification Report and Final Verification Report).
The criteria of this verification include the relevant rules and steps as set out in the VCS.
3 Verification Findings
3.1 Remaining issues, including any material discrepancy, from previous
validation
There are no pending CARs and CLs issued in the course of the validation that still
needs to be properly addressed to get closed.
4
VCS 2007.1 Verification Report
3.2 Project Implementation
The project consists of a fuel switch from non-renewable biomass to renewable biomass
in the ceramic facility. The use of non-renewable native wood will be substituted by
renewable biomass of different kinds as can be seen in the table below.
Table 3-1: Type of biomass
Renewable biomass applied Description of sustainability
Sawdust The sawdust that may be used in the project
activity is originated from sawmills industries which
utilizes wood coming from sustainable forest
management. As the sawdust is an industrial
residue it falls therefore under the renewable
biomass criteria of the applied methodology.
Sustainability will be provided by the assurance of
the quantities and origin of the sawdust used via
the check of the invoices.
Sugar cane bagasse The sugar cane production is necessary to
maintain the production of sugar and alcohol mills
in the region. Its cultivation is sustainable because
the sugar and alcohol facilities demand yearly
incoming of sugar cane to maintain the production,
thus is necessary to maintain permanently the
cultivation area. Sustainability will be provided by
the assurance of the quantities and origin of the
sugar cane bagasse used via the check of the
invoices.
Biomass that may be applied Description of sustainability
Peanut shell This biomass is a residue/waste from peanut
production. Its cultivation is assumed as
sustainable because the producers of peanuts
demand yearly processing of peanuts thus it is
necessary to maintain regularly the cultivation
area. Sao Paulo State is a huge producer of
peanut. It is a waste from the peanut production
and therefore renewable according to the
methodology. Sustainability will be provided by the
assurance of the quantities and origin of the
peanut shells used via the check of the invoices.
Elephant grass This is an alternative which is seen with potential
for the future. If adopted by the project activity, the
elephant grass cultivation should follow
sustainable management methodologies. The
elephant grass will be exclusively cultivated for the
purpose to supply fuel to the project activity. The
ceramic owner will be responsible to maintain or to
evidence the sustainability of the production. Until
now the ceramic did not use elephant grass for
their project. The evidence of sustainability will be
provided by the evidences of the sustainable
management origin of the elephant grass and by
the control of the quantities involved.

3.4 Accuracy of Emission Reduction Calculations
11
VCS 2007.1 Verification Report
The approved CDM baseline and monitoring methodology Category AMS-I.E: Switch
from non-renewable biomass for thermal applications by the user - Version 01, February
1st 2008 onwards, is applied to the project activity.
GHG emission reduction is calculated as baseline emissions minus project emissions
minus leakage emissions. As no project and leakage emissions are identified according
to the applied methodology, baseline emissions are equal to the amount of emission
reduction. Emission reduction is calculated trough the estimated value of native wood
that would be consumed in the absence of the project activity in year y (according
option “b” of applied methodology as stated in section 3.3 of this report) multiplied by
the fraction of non-renewable biomass (wood) used in the absence of the project activity
in year y multiplied by the Net Calorific Value of non-renewable biomass multiplied by
the EF of the projected fossil fuel adopted. The approach used for the emission
reduction calculation was considered to be in accordance with the applied methodology.
It was verified in the course of this verification that the above-mentioned methodology
has been correctly and accurately applied in calculating the total emission reductions
and the emission reduction calculation is accurate and conservative.

VCS 2007.1 Verification Report
4 Verification Conclusion
Carbono Social Serviços Ambienatis Ltda has commissioned the TÜV NORD JI/CDM
Certification Program to carry out the 1st periodic verification of the project “Sol Nascente
Ceramic Fuel Switching Project“”, with regard to the relevant requirements for CDM project
activities. The project reduces GHG emissions due to switching non-renewable to renewable
biomass fuel for end-user thermal energy generation. This verification covers the period from
2006-04-01 to 2008-09-30 (including both days).
In the course of the verification 8 Corrective Action Requests (CAR) and 3 Clarification
Requests (CL) were raised and successfully closed. Furthermore 0 FARs are raised to improve
the monitoring system in the future. The verification is based on the draft monitoring report,
revised monitoring report, the monitoring plan as set out in the VCS PD, the validation report,
emission reduction calculation spreadsheet and supporting documents made available to the
TÜV NORD JI/CDM CP by the project participant.
As a result of this verification, the verifier confirms that:
• all operations of the project are implemented and installed as planned and described in
the validated project design document.
• the monitoring plan is in accordance with the applied approved CDM methodology ,ie,
AMS-I.E: Switch from non-renewable biomass for thermal applications by the user -
Version 01, February 1st 2008 onwards
• the installed equipment essential for measuring parameters required for calculating
emission reductions are calibrated appropriately.
• the monitoring system is in place and functional. The project has generated GHG
emission reductions.
As the result of the 1st periodic verification, the verifier confirms that the GHG emission
reductions are calculated without material misstatements in a conservative and appropriate
manner. TÜV NORD JI/CDM CP herewith confirms that the project has achieved emission
reductions in the above mentioned reporting period as follows: Emission reductions:

Inventário 2021 - Sustainable Carbon
SUSTAINABLE CARBON - PROJETOS AMBIENTAIS LTDA.
Nome fantasia: Sustainable Carbon
CNPJ: 09.510.073/0001-06
Setor econômico: Atividades profissionais, científicas e técnicas
Subsetor: Outras atividades profissionais, científicas e técnicas
Endereço: R DOUTOR BACELAR - 368 - ANDAR 02 Conjunto 23 - Vila Clementino - São
Paulo - SP - 04026-001
Responsável pela publicação do inventário: Equipe Sustainable Carbon
(tecnica@sustainablecarbon.com)
Informações institucionais
A Sustainable Carbon é especialista e líder na América Latina no desenvolvimento de
projetos de reduções de emissões e de soluções ligadas ao gerenciamento de Gases de
Efeito Estufa, GEEs.
Nossas soluções permitem que organizações e indivíduos conheçam e reduzam seus
impactos ambientais, possibilitando o equilíbrio das emissões e contribuindo para o
desenvolvimento sustentável de comunidades no mundo todo.
Também conectamos pessoas querendo fazer a diferença no mundo: empresários,
desenvolvedores de projetos, empresas, ONGs, governos, associações e entusiastas de
todo o mundo.
Fazemos isso por meio de mecanismos técnicos e inovadores aprovados pelo mercado de
carbono que quantificam as reduções das emissões GEE. Estabelecendo, assim, um preço
sobre esse serviço ambiental e permitindo o comércio internacional desta commodity
única: o crédito de carbono.
Assim, aqueles que reduzem suas emissões recebem por isso e são capazes de manter os
projetos de carbono, que preservam ecossistemas ameaçados e geram desenvolvimento
na região onde estão localizados.
Somos especialistas no desenvolvimento de projetos de energia renovável, de
substituição de combustível, compostagem, projetos de biomassa e projetos florestais.
Realizamos trabalhos em quase todos os ecossistemas brasileiros. Foi assim que nos
últimos anos a Sustainable Carbon entregou créditos de carbono da mais alta qualidade
para compensações e hoje é a empresa líder neste segmento na América Latina.
Veja abaixo nossas conquistas:
Somos a empresa líder mundial em desenvolvimento de projetos para o mercado
voluntário (VER), gerenciando um portfólio com mais de 50 projetos validados e mais de
16M de toneladas em reduções de emissões;
Pioneira no desenvolvimento de melhores práticas sustentáveis, validamos o primeiro
projeto SOCIALCARBON® em 2008 e o primeiro projeto Gold Standard para o mercado
voluntário no Brasil em 2011;
Mais de 50 comunidades já foram beneficiadas através dos créditos de carbono gerados
para o desenvolvimento sustentável;
Soluções de GEEs para mais de 100 empresas em 23 países.

4. Outros Elementos
Campos de preenchimento opcional
4.1 Informações sobre a performance da organização, em comparação com benchmarks internos (ex: outras
unidades) ou externos (ex: organizações do mesmo setor).
As emissões relatadas no Inventário da Sustainable Carbon para este ano apresentaram relativos aumentos nos
escopos 2 e 3. Isso se deve principalmente à pandemia de Covid-19, desde março de 2020, que fez com que todas
as atividades da empresa fossem realizadas em sistema de teletrabalho. Porém, no ano de 2021 as atividades
presenciais foram aos poucos sendo autorizadas. Dessa forma, houve consumo de energia elétrica no escritório
nos dias em que foi possível realizar atividades presenciais e viagens de deslocamento casa-trabalho dos
funcionários. "Viagens a Negócios" ainda corresponde à maior fonte de emissões do escopo 3, com o número de
viagens aumentando comparado ao último inventário. Para o ano de 2021, também foram reportadas emissões de
energia elétrica no escopo 3, correspondentes ao consumo dos funcionários durante o expediente de trabalho em
casa, tendo como referência o consumo no escritório durante o ano de 2019, por ser o último ano com atividades
100% presenciais.
4.2 Descrição de indicadores de emissão de GEE para as atividades da organização. Por exemplo, tCO e/produtos
2
fabricados.
Não foi relatado.
4.3 Descrição de estratégias e projetos para a gestão de emissões de GEE.
Uma vez que grande parte das emissões estão alocadas no escopo 3, a Sustainable Carbon trabalha com algumas
medidas e princípios para diminuir as emissões ao longo do tempo. É prática comum da empresa: realizar reuniões
via vídeo conferência, evitando assim o deslocamento de ida e volta até o local da reunião. Quando realiza visita de
verificação dos projetos, opta-se por fazer a visita de um polo inteiro, passando por todos os projetos do Estado de
uma única vez, utilizando assim o transporte aéreo em apenas uma ida e volta. Durante o ano de 2021 essas ações
tiveram ainda mais importância, visando reduzir a exposição dos funcionários ao vírus e incentivando a vacinação.
As Emissões aumentaram ligeiramente em relação a 2020, pois devido às campanhas de imunização foi possível
realizar visitas ao escritório e aos projetos, mas o teletrabalho permanece o regime majoritário da empresa.
4.4 Informações sobre contratos com clientes e fornecedores que incluam cláusulas vinculadas à elaboração de
inventários de GEE e/ou ao envio de informações relacionadas.
Não foi relatado.
4.5 Informações sobre incertezas, exclusões de fontes de dados e outras características da elaboração do
inventário.
A administração do condomínio é responsável pelo controle da energia elétrica adquirida para a área comum do
prédio, essas emissões foram excluídas do inventário de 2021, por dificuldade na coleta de dados e por serem
irrelevantes. Para o ano de 2021, também foram reportadas emissões de energia elétrica no escopo 3,
correspondentes ao consumo dos funcionários durante o expediente de trabalho em casa, tendo como referência o
consumo no escritório durante o ano de 2019, por ter sido o último ano com atividades 100% presenciais.

4.6 Descrição sobre ações internas para melhoria da qualidade do inventário de GEE. Por exemplo, sistematização
da coleta de dados, contratação de verificação externa, etc.
A Sustainable Carbon desenvolve sistematização de coleta de dados que abrange todo o escopo de suas atividades
organizacionais, através de ferramentas corporativas e a difusão interna do conhecimento sobre as emissões de
GEE. Para a coleta interna dos dados, criamos uma plataforma com acesso a todos os colaboradores. Este
instrumento possibilita a coleta dos dados assim que ocorre a atividade e sua emissão, facilitando o monitoramento
dos indicadores, realização do inventário e também o processo de verificação por terceira parte.
Assim como os instrumentos corporativos, o conhecimento compartilhado junto aos colaboradores mostra-se
importante para a qualidade e transparência do inventário. Todos os colaboradores passam por orientações quanto
ao fornecimento dos dados necessários à elaboração do inventário, assim como a realização de treinamentos
internos, visando à difusão de conhecimento sobre os inventários de GEE.
