VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
Summary:
Bureau Veritas Certification has made the 2ndmonitoring period verification of GE Teobaldo Ceramic
Fuel Switching Project, located in the State of Pernambuco, in Brazil, in the municipality of Paudalho.
The project activity is the project of GE Teobaldo Ceramic, which is a red ceramic company located in
Paudalho municipality, in the state of Pernambuco, northeast region of Brazil. The ceramic company
produces bricks and flagstones, destined mainly for the regional market in Pernambuco.
The objective of the implementation of this project activity in the ceramic is to stop using the native
wood, which is a non-renewable fuel, and completely substitute it with renewable biomasses to feed
their kilns and fire their ceramic units, so that they can minimize environmental impacts related to the
native wood deforestation and consumption. This fuel switching project is reducing the greenhouse gas
(GHG) emissions through the substitution of native wood for renewable biomasses to generate thermal
energy.
The verification scope is defined as a periodic independent and objective review and an ex-post
determination of the monitored reductions in GHG emissions during defined verification period and
consisted of the following three phases: i) desk review of the project design, baseline and monitoring
plan; ii) follow-up interviews with project stakeholders: iii) resolution of outstanding issues and the
issuance of the final verification report and opinion. The overall verification, from Contract Review to
Verification Report & Opinion, was conducted using Bureau Veritas Certification internal procedures.
The first output of the verification process is a list of Clarification and Corrective Actions Requests (CLs
and CARs), presented in the Verification Report (Section 7). Taking into account this output, the project
proponent revised its Monitoring Report Document, version 03.0.
In summary, it is Bureau Veritas Certification’s opinion that the project correctly applies the Small Scale
CDM Methodology “Switch from Non-Renewable Biomass for Thermal Applications by the User” –
AMS I.E, version 1, and meets the relevant VCS version 3 requirements and local legislation.
During this verification, 1 Corrective Action Request (CAR) and 5 Clarification Requests (CLs) were
raised and successfully closed.
As the result of the 2nd periodic verification, the verifier confirms that the GHG emission reductions are
calculated without material misstatements in a conservative and appropriate manner. Bureau Veritas
Certification herewith confirms that the project has achieved emission reductions in the above
mentioned reporting period as follows:
Reporting period: 01-May-2010 to 29-February-2012

1 INTRODUCTION
1.1 Objective
Sustainable Carbon Projetos Ambientais has commissioned Bureau Veritas Certification (BVC) to verify
the emission reductions of its VCS Project “GE Teobaldo Ceramic Fuel Switching Project”.
This report summarizes the findings of the second periodic verification of the project, performed on the
basis of VCS – Verified Carbon Standard version 3.4 criteria, as well as criteria given to provide for
consistent project operations, monitoring and reporting.
Verification is the periodic independent review and ex-post determination of the monitored reductions in
(greenhouse Gas) GHG emissions during defined verification period.
The objective of the periodic verification is to verify that actual monitoring systems and procedures are in
compliance with the monitoring systems and procedures described in the monitoring plan; furthermore,
the periodic verification evaluates the GHG emission reductions data and express a conclusion with a
high, but not absolute, level of assurance about whether the reported GHG emission data is free of
material misstatements; and verifies that the reported GHG emission reductions data is sufficiently
supported by evidence, i.e. monitoring records.
The verification considers both quantitative and qualitative information on emission reductions.
Quantitative data comprises the monitoring reports submitted to the verifier by the project entity.
Qualitative data comprises information on internal management controls, calculation procedures and
procedures for transfer, frequency of emissions reports and review of calculations/data transfers.
1.2 Scope and Criteria
The verification scope is defined as an independent and objective review of the project description, the
project’s baseline study and monitoring plan and other relevant documents. The information in these
documents is reviewed against VCS – Verified Carbon Standard version 3.3.
The verification is not meant to provide any consulting towards the Client. However, stated requests for
forward actions and/or corrective actions may provide input for improvement of the project monitoring
towards reductions in the GHG emissions.
1.3 Level of assurance
During the verification process, the GHG team assessed the monitoring report, the calculation
spreadsheets and supporting documents.
Based on this assessment, the verification team can express a reasonable level of assurance, stating that
the GHG emission reductions:
- Are materially correct and are a fair representation of the GHG data, and
- Are prepared in accordance with the relevant International Standard on GHG quantification,
monitoring and reporting, and with relevant national standards.

The verification report is based on VCS Monitoring Report (MR) version 02.0, of 06/12/2012, and other
documents provided by the project proponent (PP), as well as, on information obtained from the on-site
visit (on 28/08/2012). The verification opinion is assured by the credibility of all the statements above.
1.4 Summary Description of the Project
GE Teobaldo Ceramic is a red ceramic company localized in Paudalho municipality, in the state of
Pernambuco, northeast region of Brazil. The ceramic company produces bricks and blocks, destined
mainly for the regional market in Pernambuco.
According to the MR (version 02.0), the objective of the implementation of this project activity is to stop
the use of native wood, which is a non-renewable fuel, and completely substitute it with renewable
biomasses to feed their kilns and fire their ceramic units, so that they can minimize environmental impacts
related to the native wood deforestation and consumption.
This fuel switching project reduces GHG emissions through the substitution of native wood for renewable
biomasses to generate thermal energy. In opposition to the identified baseline, the project activity
generates thermal energy without stimulating deforestation and uses abundant renewable biomasses.
BVC was able to validate the project descriptions as described above and in the MR (version 02.0), by
observations and interviews carried out during site visit (on 28/08/2012) and by analyzing the project’s PD
and Validation Report (available on VCS Project database online (http://www.vcsprojectdatabase.org/).
The emission reductions due to the substitution of native wood for renewable biomasses to generate
thermal energy by GE Teobaldo Ceramic resulted in 9,873 tCO e, during this 2nd monitoring period, from
2
01-May-2010 to 29-February-2012. Please see Sections 3 and 4 below for a description on how BVC was
able to validate and confirm this amount of emission reductions.
2 VALIDATION PROCESS, FINDINGS AND CONCLUSION
2.1 Validation Process
The validation process is described in the VCS Validation Report, which can be accessed on the VCS
Project database online (http://www.vcsprojectdatabase.org/). Please refer to the PD and Validation
Report included in the VCS Project database. Please see below for some details regarding the validation
process:
- Method and Criteria:
According to the Validation Report (please see VCS Project database online), the validation scope is
defined as an independent and objective review of the project design document, the project’s baseline
study and monitoring plan and other relevant documents. The information in these documents is reviewed
against Kyoto Protocol requirements, VCS rules and associated interpretations. For additional
information, please refer to the VCS Project database online (http://www.vcsprojectdatabase.org/).
- Document Review:

According to the Validation Report, the VCS PD as well as other supporting documents were reviewed.
Furthermore, the validation team used additional documentation by third parties, such as host party
legislation and technical data based on the project’s design. The validation report shows the documents
considered during the validation process, such as: documents provided by the project proponent,
background documents and websites consulted. For additional information, please refer to the VCS
Project database online (http://www.vcsprojectdatabase.org/).
- Interviews:
According to the validation report, on June 10th, 2009, Bureau Veritas Certification performed interviews
with persons of GE Teobaldo Ceramic and Social Carbon Ltda. (now Sustainable Carbon Projetos
Ambientais), related to project specific questions and emission factor. For additional information, please
refer to the VCS Project database online (http://www.vcsprojectdatabase.org/).
Site Inspections:
According to the validation report, a site inspection was carried out during the validation process on
10/06/2009. For additional information, please refer to the VCS Project database online
(http://www.vcsprojectdatabase.org/).
Resolution of Any Material Discrepancy:
The validation report includes Corrective Action and Clarification Requests (CARs and CRs) identified in
the course of the validation. A Corrective Action Request is established if: mistakes have been made in
assumptions or the project documentation which directly will influence the project results, the
requirements deemed relevant for validation of the project with certain characteristics have not been met
or there is a risk that the project would not be registered by the VCS or that emission reductions cannot
be verified and certified. A Clarification Request is issued where information is insufficient, unclear or not
transparent enough to establish whether a requirement is met.
After resolution of these CARs and CRs by the project proponent, the validator issued the (final)
validation report and opinion.41 CARs and 40 CRs were found during pre-validation. Please refer to
Section 3 of the Validation Report. For additional information, please refer to the VCS Project database
online (http://www.vcsprojectdatabase.org/).

2.3 Validation Conclusion
The validation conclusion is described in the VCS Validation Report, which can be accessed on the VCS
Project database online (http://www.vcsprojectdatabase.org/). Please refer to the PD and Validation
Report included in the VCS Project database. Please see below for details regarding the validation
conclusion:
According to the validation report: By generating energy using renewable biomass, the project is likely to
result in reductions of GHG emissions partially displacing the energy that would have been generated
using fossil fuels. An analysis of the investment barrier demonstrates that the proposed project activity is
not a likely baseline scenario. Emission reductions attributable to the project are hence additional to any
that would occur in the absence of the project activity. Given that the project is implemented and
maintained as designed, the project is likely to achieve the estimated amount of emission reductions.
3 VERIFICATION PROCESS
3.1 Method and Criteria
The second verification comprises the period from 01-May-2010 to 29-February-2012. This verification
report was prepared between 16-August-2012 and 27-December-2012. This period encompass the site
visit preparation, site visit, preliminary report and final report.
The criteria of this verification include the relevant rules and steps as set out in the VCS version 3.4. The
verification process includes:
 A desk review of the VCS PD and supporting documents with the use of the relevant sections of a
customized protocol;
 A desk review of the Monitoring Report (MR) and additional supporting documents which the
client submitted;
 On-Site assessment on 28/08/2012;
 Background investigation and follow-up interviews with personnel of the project developer;
 Verification reporting (Draft Verification Report and Final Verification Report).
3.2 Document Review
The document review was conducted based on a desk review of the Monitoring Report (MR), calculation
spreadsheets and additional supporting documents submitted by the client. The relevant sections of the
above-mentioned customized protocol were used (See Section 7 below). Also, the desk review of the
VCS PD, supporting documents and CDM methodology AMS I.E (version 1) was carried out using the
relevant sections of a customized protocol.
All the relevant information and evidences regarding project description and monitoring process were
assessed and validated by the verification team during the site visit and documentation review. The
reference documents are listed below:
• Final VCS Project Description (Version 03), dated 02/10/2009.
• Validation Report, dated 20/11/2009.

3.3 Interviews
On 28/08/2012, a site visit was made, when Bureau Veritas Certification performed interviews with the
project proponents to confirm selected information and to resolve issues identified in the document

Teobaldo Ceramic (Project Proponent) were interviewed. The main topics of the interviews were:
 Project Design and implementation;
 Technical Equipment and operation;
 Equipment calibration;
 Monitoring Plan;
 Monitored data;
 Data uncertainty and residual risks;
 GHG Calculation;
 Environmental Impacts;
 Compliance with National Laws and regulations;
 Environmental Licenses.
3.4 Site Inspections
On-site assessment was conducted on 28/08/2012, in order to crosscheck all the relevant information and
data provided by the Project Proponents, against to the physical documentation and registers, as well as
to assess the project description, as per the MR and PD, to that observed in the field.

3.5 Resolution of Any Material Discrepancy
During the verification process, corrective action requests (CARs) and clarification requests (CLs)
identified by the verifier were raised in the verification protocol and submitted to the PP to be addressed
before the conclusion of the verification process.
The resolution of CARs and CLs were carried out by the PP through adjustments in the project
documentation, especially in the MR and calculation spreadsheet. In some cases, these adjustments
were supported by evidences, additional documentation or technical explanation. No FARs (Forward
Action Requests) were raised during the previous (first) verification process.
The objective of this phase of the verification is to raise the requests for corrective actions and
clarification and any other outstanding issues that needed to be clarified for Bureau Veritas Certification
positive conclusion on the GHG emission reduction calculation.
Findings established during the initial verification can either be seen as a non-fulfillment of criteria
ensuring the proper implementation of a project or where a risk to deliver high quality emission reductions
is identified.
Corrective Action Requests (CAR) is issued, where:
(a) Non-conformities with the monitoring plan or methodology are found in monitoring and reporting, or if
the evidence provided to prove conformity is insufficient;
(b) Mistakes have been made in applying assumptions, data or calculations of emission reductions, which
would impair the estimate of emission reductions.
The Clarification Request (CL) is used if information is insufficient or not clear enough to determine
whether the applicable VCS version 3 requirements have been met. During this (second) verification
process, no FARs (Forward Action Requests) were raised.
During this verification, 01 Corrective Action Request (CAR) and 05 Clarification Requests (CL) were
raised and successfully closed.
To guarantee the transparency of the verification process, the concerns raised are documented in more
detail in the verification protocol found in the Section 7, below.
4 VERIFICATION FINDINGS

4.2 Accuracy of GHG Emission Reduction or Removal Calculations

procedures of the applied CDM methodology: AMS-I.E (version 01).The project activity in this monitoring

14,230.46 MWh per year, which corresponds to the use of 1.62 MWthermal on average of the kilns
capacity during the monitored period, which is less than the limits of 45 MWthermal for Type I Small scale
project activities. According to the MR, baseline emissions are calculated as follows:

The Category AMS-I.E predicts the following possible three sources of leakage:
A) If the project activity includes substitution of non-renewable biomass by renewable biomass, leakage in
the production of renewable biomass must be considered.
The leakage from biomass projects, like this project activity, should also be estimated according to the
“General guidance on leakage in biomass project activities” (attachment C of appendix B) of Indicative
Simplified Baseline and Monitoring Methodologies for Selected Small-Scale CDM Project Activity
Categories, which identifies different emission sources based on the type of biomass considered. Please
refer to Table 2 in the Section 4.3 of the MR version 02.0 (Sources of leakage according to the type of the
biomass).
Still according to the MR (version 02.0), the possible sources of leakage of the present project activity is
the competing use of (1) Algaroba wood, (2) Native wood with sustainable forest management plan, (3)
Wood residues and (4) Sugar cane briquette. According to the MR (version 02.0), all these biomasses
have enough availability, confirming the non-existence of leakage for each biomass type. Please refer to
Section 4.3 of the MR (version 02.0), where PP describes the abundance of these types of biomass in the
project area, demonstrating that non-existence leakage in the production of renewable biomass.
(B)Leakage relating to the non-renewable biomass shall be assessed from ex-post surveys of users and
areas from where biomass is sourced.
According to the MR (version 02.0), three types of leaked were identified, which relate to type (B)
leakage. Please refer to item (B) of Section 4.3 of the MR (version 02.0). According to the PP, the project
will contribute to reduce the use of non-renewable biomass, especially due to the incentive of carbon
credits. Therefore, it can be concluded that this source of leakage (type B), until the date of this
monitoring report, is not considered for this project activity. This leakage was monitored in order to
guarantee the project conservativeness.
(C)If the equipment is transferred from another activity or if the existing equipment is transferred to
another activity, leakage is to be considered.
According to the MR (version 02.0), this type of leakage is not applicable for this project activity as there
was no transference of equipment, since new equipments had to be acquired.
BVC was able to validate PP’s explanation regarding the non-existence of leakage in this 2ndmonitoring
period by: observations during site visit (28/08/2012), interviews carried out during site visit,
crosschecking (online) references provided by PP in the MR (version 02.0) and by analyzing documents
of the first verification procedure and validation, online available at http://www.vcsprojectdatabase.org/.
4.3 Quality of Evidence to Determine GHG Emission Reductions or Removals
The evidences used to determine GHG emission reductions were:
• Monitoring Report – second monitoring period (version 02.0), dated 06/12/2012.
• VCS MR Calculations – GE Teobaldo_period_ 02_01 05 10_29 02 12_v.02.0.xls.
• Ceramic’s environmental production license, LO (Operation License): 05.09.06.002468-6, valid
until 11/06/2011.
• Ceramic’s environmental production license LO: 05.11.07.004439-4, valid until 26/07/2012.

dated 04/11/2011.
• Environmental clay extraction License LO: 00003/2009 Valid until 07/01/2011.
• Environmental clay extraction License LO: 05.11.03.002842-6, valid until 30/03/2013.
• “Forestry Origin Document” (in Portuguese, DOF – Documento de Origem Florestal), for the
entire monitoring period from 01-May-2010 to 29-February-2012.
• Renewable biomasses receipts and invoices, covering the entire 2nd monitoring period.
• Production documents (internal control sheet monitored by the project proponent: Production
spreadsheet; Daily machine production and Monthly production reports), covering the entire 2nd
monitoring period.
All Renewable biomasses receipts and invoices (100%) were crosschecked by the verification team. No
discrepancies were found between receipts/invoices and version 02.0 of the MR and calculation
spreadsheet version 02.0. Seeing this, the verification team confirms that the evidences used to
determine GHG Emission Reductions are reliable, sufficient and appropriate for the determination of GHG
emission reductions.
4.4 Management and Operational System
According to Section 3.3 of the MR (version 02.0), the monitoring is done with the aim of determining the
most approximate quantity of non-renewable wood that, in the absence of the project, would be used in
the ceramics’ kilns and consequently the amount of GHG that would be emitted in tonnes of CO2e.
Ceramic personnel are responsible to monitor the operating conditions on the factory, including the
amount of renewable biomass consumed and the monthly production of ceramic devices (parameters
Q and PR ). Ceramic personnel are also responsible to store invoices, receipts of sales and other
renbiomass y
documents related to purchase or acquisition of biomass.
Based on this information, Sustainable Carbon is responsible to assess if the biomasses are from
renewable origin, to evaluate if there is a surplus of renewable biomass and to calculate emission
reductions, including an assessment of leakage emissions and the determination of parameter ƒ .
NRB,y
Sustainable Carbon also assists the Ceramic personnel to double check the monitored data on biomass
consumption and ceramic devices production. No internal auditing was performed on data related to this
monitoring period. Non-conformities identified in the double check procedures are clarified between
Sustainable Carbon technical team and the monitoring responsible on the ceramic during site visits for
data collection, by reassessing existing documents and sources of information.
The responsible for the monitoring plan are Mrs. Ana Carla de O. S. Machado, Luciana Maria de Lira and
Mr. Joaquim Beltrão Correia de Oliveira from GE Teobaldo Ceramic. Sustainable Carbon technical
(including those members described on Section 1.3 of the MR) were also involved in the project
monitoring.
The responsibilities and authorities for monitoring and reporting have been found to be in accordance with
the monitoring plan, included in the registered PD.
5 VERIFICATION CONCLUSION
Sustainable Carbon Projetos Ambientais has commissioned Bureau Veritas Certification to carry out the
2nd periodic verification of the GE Teobaldo Ceramic Fuel Switching Project with regard to the relevant
v3.2 22
VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
requirements for VCS project activities. The objective of the implementation of this project activity in the
ceramic is to stop the use of native wood, which is a non-renewable fuel, and completely substitute it with
renewable biomasses to feed their kilns and fire their ceramic units, so that they can minimize
environmental impacts related to the native wood deforestation and consumption.
In the course of the verification, Corrective Action Requests (CARs) and Clarification Requests (CLs)
were raised and successfully closed. The verification conclusion is based on the Monitoring Report
(version 03.0, dated 14-01-2013), the VCS PD (version 3, dated02/10/2009), Emission Reduction
Calculation spreadsheet version 03.0 and supporting documents made available to Bureau Veritas
Certification by the project participant.
As a result of this verification, BVC confirms that:
• Bureau Veritas Certification confirms that the project is implemented as planned and described in the
Project Description. The monitoring plan is in place and the project is generating GHG emission
reductions. BVC concludes that all operations of the project are implemented and installed as planned
and described as in the validated project description;
• The monitoring plan is in accordance with the applied approved CDM methodology, i.e., AMS I.E
(version 1);
• The monitoring system is in place and functional. The project has generated GHG emission reductions.
Bureau Veritas Certification confirms that Monitoring Report version 03.0 and Emission Reduction
Calculation spreadsheet, version 03.0 are in accordance with the registered monitoring plan and all the
calculations were based in reliable monitored data. In the case of non-monitored and pre-defined
parameters, PP has correctly followed the parameters stated in the validated VCS PD, version 3, dated
02/10/2009.
BVC also confirms that all the data and information used to elaborate this Verification Report are treated
confidentially by Bureau Veritas Certification.

SUSTAINABLE CARBON - PROJETOS AMBIENTAIS LTDA.
Nome fantasia: Sustainable Carbon

Endereço: R DOUTOR BACELAR - 368 - ANDAR 02 Conjunto 23 - Vila Clementino - São
Paulo - SP - 04026-001
Responsável pela publicação do inventário: Equipe Sustainable Carbon
(tecnica@sustainablecarbon.com)
Informações institucionais
A Sustainable Carbon é especialista e líder na América Latina no desenvolvimento de
projetos de reduções de emissões e de soluções ligadas ao gerenciamento de Gases de
Efeito Estufa, GEEs.
Nossas soluções permitem que organizações e indivíduos conheçam e reduzam seus
impactos ambientais, possibilitando o equilíbrio das emissões e contribuindo para o
desenvolvimento sustentável de comunidades no mundo todo.
Também conectamos pessoas querendo fazer a diferença no mundo: empresários,
desenvolvedores de projetos, empresas, ONGs, governos, associações e entusiastas de
todo o mundo.
Fazemos isso por meio de mecanismos técnicos e inovadores aprovados pelo mercado de
carbono que quantificam as reduções das emissões GEE. Estabelecendo, assim, um preço
sobre esse serviço ambiental e permitindo o comércio internacional desta commodity
única: o crédito de carbono.
Assim, aqueles que reduzem suas emissões recebem por isso e são capazes de manter os
projetos de carbono, que preservam ecossistemas ameaçados e geram desenvolvimento
na região onde estão localizados.
Somos especialistas no desenvolvimento de projetos de energia renovável, de
substituição de combustível, compostagem, projetos de biomassa e projetos florestais.
Realizamos trabalhos em quase todos os ecossistemas brasileiros. Foi assim que nos
últimos anos a Sustainable Carbon entregou créditos de carbono da mais alta qualidade
para compensações e hoje é a empresa líder neste segmento na América Latina.
Veja abaixo nossas conquistas:
Somos a empresa líder mundial em desenvolvimento de projetos para o mercado
voluntário (VER), gerenciando um portfólio com mais de 50 projetos validados e mais de
16M de toneladas em reduções de emissões;
Pioneira no desenvolvimento de melhores práticas sustentáveis, validamos o primeiro
projeto SOCIALCARBON® em 2008 e o primeiro projeto Gold Standard para o mercado
voluntário no Brasil em 2011;
Mais de 50 comunidades já foram beneficiadas através dos créditos de carbono gerados
para o desenvolvimento sustentável;
Soluções de GEEs para mais de 100 empresas em 23 países.

4. Outros Elementos
Campos de preenchimento opcional
4.1 Informações sobre a performance da organização, em comparação com benchmarks internos (ex: outras
unidades) ou externos (ex: organizações do mesmo setor).
As emissões relatadas no Inventário da Sustainable Carbon para este ano apresentaram relativo aumento no
escopo 3 devido ao aumento na demanda de viagens a negócio. No entanto, em relação as emissões referente ao
deslocamento casa-trabalho, houve diminuição nas emissões, isso se deve principalmente a adoção do regime de
trabalho remoto, adotado pela maioria dos colaboradores neste ano. Pelo mesmo motivo, ocorreu a diminuição nas
emissões de Escopo 2 atrelado a um menor consumo de energia elétrica, seguindo a abordagem por localização.
"Viagens a Negócios" ainda corresponde à maior fonte de emissões do escopo 3, com o número de viagens
aumentando comparado ao último inventário. Para o ano de 2022, também foram reportadas emissões de energia
elétrica no escopo 3, correspondentes ao consumo dos funcionários durante o expediente de trabalho em casa,
tendo como referência o consumo no escritório durante o ano de 2019, por ser o último ano com atividades 100%
presenciais.
4.2 Descrição de indicadores de emissão de GEE para as atividades da organização. Por exemplo, tCO e/produtos
2
fabricados.
Não foi relatado.
4.3 Descrição de estratégias e projetos para a gestão de emissões de GEE.
Uma vez que grande parte das emissões estão alocadas no escopo 3, a Sustainable Carbon trabalha com algumas
medidas e princípios para diminuir as emissões ao longo do tempo. É prática comum da empresa: realizar reuniões
via vídeo conferência, evitando assim o deslocamento de ida e volta até o local da reunião. Quando realiza visita de
verificação dos projetos, opta-se por fazer a visita de um polo inteiro, passando por todos os projetos do Estado de
uma única vez, utilizando assim o transporte aéreo em apenas uma ida e volta. As Emissões aumentaram
ligeiramente em relação a 2021, devido a retomada das atividades pós período de pandemia, onde priorizou-se a
atualização das verificações não realizadas durante o período pandêmico.
4.4 Informações sobre contratos com clientes e fornecedores que incluam cláusulas vinculadas à elaboração de
inventários de GEE e/ou ao envio de informações relacionadas.

4.5 Informações sobre incertezas, exclusões de fontes de dados e outras características da elaboração do
inventário.
A administração do condomínio é responsável pelo controle da energia elétrica adquirida para a área comum do
prédio, recarga de extintores. Essas emissões foram excluídas do inventário de 2022, por dificuldade na coleta de
dados e por serem irrelevantes. Outro dado informado pela administração do condomínio, foi a estimativa do
consumo de óleo diesel para a geração de energia pelo gerador do prédio. Para o ano de 2022, também foram
reportadas emissões de energia elétrica no escopo 3, correspondentes ao consumo dos funcionários durante o
expediente de trabalho em casa, tendo como referência o consumo no escritório durante o ano de 2019, por ter
sido o último ano com atividades 100% presenciais. As emissões ligadas a manutenção e recarga dos aparelhos de
ar-condicionado não foram reportadas no ano de 2022, já que não houve necessidade por esse serviço neste ano.

4.6 Descrição sobre ações internas para melhoria da qualidade do inventário de GEE. Por exemplo, sistematização
da coleta de dados, contratação de verificação externa, etc.
A Sustainable Carbon desenvolve sistematização de coleta de dados que abrange todo o escopo de suas atividades
organizacionais, através de ferramentas corporativas e a difusão interna do conhecimento sobre as emissões de
GEE. Para a coleta interna dos dados, criamos uma plataforma com acesso a todos os colaboradores. Este
instrumento possibilita a coleta dos dados assim que ocorre a atividade e sua emissão, facilitando o monitoramento
dos indicadores, realização do inventário e também o processo de verificação por terceira parte.
Assim como os instrumentos corporativos, o conhecimento compartilhado junto aos colaboradores mostra-se
importante para a qualidade e transparência do inventário. Todos os colaboradores passam por orientações quanto
ao fornecimento dos dados necessários à elaboração do inventário, assim como a realização de treinamentos
internos, visando à difusão de conhecimento sobre os inventários de GEE.

VCS 2007 Verification Report Template
BUREAU VERITAS CERTIFICATION has made the verification of the Irmãos
Fredi Ceramic Fuel Switching Project, which is is located in the city
of Presidente Epitácio, São Paulo State, Brazil. Irmãos Fredi Ceramic
is an enterprise that produces structural ceramic units. The project
activity consists on utilizing sugar cane bagasse and a mix of
sawdust and woodchips to feed the kilns other than using native wood
from the Cerrado forest, which was a pioneer practice in the region.
The verification scope is defined as a periodic independent and
objective review and an ex-post determination of the monitored
reductions in GHG emissions during defined verification period and
consisted of the following three phases:
i) desk review of the project design, baseline and monitoring plan;
ii) follow-up interviews with project stakeholders;
iii) resolution of outstanding issues and the issuance of the final
verification report and opinion.
The overall verification, from Contract Review to verification Report
& Opinion, was conducted using BUREAU VERITAS CERTIFICATION internal
procedures.
The first output of the verification process is a list of
Clarification and Corrective Actions Requests (CL and CAR), and
Forward Action Requests (FAR), presented in the Verification
Protocol, at the Section 7 from this Verification Report. Taking into
account this output, the project proponent revised its Monitoring
Report Document, originating the MR version 03 (latest edition of the
MR), of September 14th, 2010.
In summary, it is BUREAU VERITAS CERTIFICATION’S opinion that the
project correctly applies the baseline and monitoring AMS-I.E (Switch
from Non – Renewable Biomass for Thermal Application by the User)
Version 01, valid from February 01st 2008 to April 08th, 2010, and
meets the relevant VCS requirements, and local legislation.
As the result of the 3rd periodic verification, the Verifier confirms
that the GHG emission reductions are calculated without material
misstatements in a conservative and appropriate manner. BUREAU
VERITAS CERTIFICATION herewith confirms that the project has achieved
emission reductions in the above mentioned reporting period as
follows:

VCS 2007 Verification Report Template
1 Introduction
Sustainable Carbon Climate Solutions has commissioned
BUREAU VERITAS Certification to verify the emission
reductions of its VCS “Irmãos Fredi Ceramic Fuel
Switching Project”.
This report summarizes the findings of the third
periodic verification of the project, performed on the
basis of VCS – Voluntary Carbon Standard version 2007.1
criteria, as well as criteria given to provide for
consistent project operations, monitoring and reporting.
1.1 Objective
Verification is the periodic independent review and ex
post determination of the monitored reductions in GHG
emissions during a defined verification period.
The objective of the periodic verification is to verify
that the actual monitoring systems and procedures are in
compliance with the monitoring systems and procedures
described in the monitoring plan; furthermore, the
periodic verification evaluates the GHG emission
reductions data and express a conclusion with a high, but
not absolute, level of assurance about whether the
reported GHG emission data is free of material
misstatements; and verifies that the reported GHG
emission reductions data is sufficiently supported by
evidence, i.e. monitoring records.
The verification shall consider both quantitative and
qualitative information on emission reductions.
Quantitative data comprises the monitoring reports
submitted to the verifier by the project entity.
Qualitative data comprises information on internal
management controls, calculation procedures and
procedures for transfer, frequency of emissions reports,
review and internal audit of calculations/data transfers.
The verification is based on criteria set by VCS –
Voluntary Carbon Standard version 2007.1.
1.2 Scope and Criteria
The verification scope is defined as an independent and
objective review of the project design document, the
project’s baseline study and monitoring plan and other
4
VCS 2007 Verification Report Template
relevant documents. The information in these documents is
reviewed against VCS – Voluntary Carbon Standard version
2007.1.
The verification is not meant to provide any consulting
towards the Client. However, stated requests for forward
actions and/or corrective actions may provide input for
improvement of the project monitoring towards reductions
in the GHG emissions.
1.3 VCS project Description
Irmãos Fredi Ceramic is an enterprise that produces
structural ceramic units. This ceramic amongst many other
ceramic industries in the region have used during many
years native wood from the Cerrado forest, in order to
supply the required amount of heat for the ceramic kilns,
this reality has aggravated the deforestation of this
biome.
The plant used to consume about 24,000 tons of native
wood from Cerrado per year to feed the kilns and maintain
a temperature of 900ºC for 50 hours producing around
750,000 roof tiles and 200,000 ceramic blocks per month.
The firewood used in the ceramic was mainly supplied by
the state of Mato Grosso do Sul which is located in the
mid-eastern region of Brazil.
This project activity reduces the greenhouse gas
emissions and obtains carbon credits through the
substitution of the thermal energy generation. In
opposition to the identified baseline scenario, this
project activity generates thermal energy without
utilizing existing carbon pool.
If nowadays the plant still would use native firewood its
consumption would be around 1,710 tons of native firewood
per month to produce 1,200,000 ceramic blocks, but since
the ceramic is no longer using native firewood in its
burning process the plant now has a monthly consumption
of about 1,400m³ of sugar cane bagasse and 1,600 m3 a mix
of sawdust and wood chips.
Therefore, the project scenario is based on the
utilization of renewable biomasses to feed the kilns
rather than using wood originated from areas without
sustainable forest management, which was a pioneer
practice in the region. The fuel switch was only feasible
when considering the revenue coming from the
commercialization of the carbon credits
1.4 Level of assurance

verification statement expressing a reasonable level of
assurance is expressed as follows:
- is materially correct and is a fair representation
of the GHG data and,
- was prepared in accordance with the related
International Standard on GHG quantification, monitoring
and reporting, and to relevant national standards.
The verification report is based on VCS PD and documents
provided by the project proponent, as well as information
obtained from the on-site visit. The verification opinion
is assured provided the credibility of all above.
2 Methodology
This is the third verification from the project activity,
covering the period from January 01st, 2010 to July 31st,
2010.
The steps carried out in this Verification were:
Preparation: August 12th, 2010. This step comprised a
previous analysis of the related documentation of the
project activity, including: Registered VCS PD,
Validation report, and previous Verifications Reports,
Monitoring Report and Calculations of the Emission
reductions.
On-site visit: August 18th, 2010. A site was conducted to
confirm the information on the Monitoring Report,
crosschecking them with the ceramic records. During the
site visit, the Verifier confirms that the project
activity is implemented and operational as described in
the registered VCS PD, by a document and record analysis
and interviews with the project responsible.
Draft Reporting: August 23rd and 24th, 2010. In this step
is prepared the Verification Protocol, on which are
stated the conclusions related to this Monitoring Period,
and the related CAR’s, CL’s or FAR’s, if applicable.

spreadsheet.

3.1 Remaining issues, including any material discrepancy,
from previous validation
7
VCS 2007 Verification Report Template
There are no pending CARs and CLs issued in the course of

3.2 Project Implementation
The implementation status of the project is as follows:
The project has a 10 years crediting period, twice
renewable.
The first 10 years crediting period is from April 01st,
2006 to March 31st, 2016.
The total Emission Reductions forecasted for that period
are 289,903 tCO .
2e
The following Verifications have already been made:
• First and Initial Periodic Verification: From April
01st, 2006 to September 30th, 2008. Emission Reductions of
58,879 tCO2e.
• Second Periodic Verification: From October 01st, 2008 to
December 31st, 2009. Emission Reductions of 30,406 tCO2e.
Project starting date: Decembre 01st, 2004.
Crediting Period Starting date: April 01st, 2006.
The project is being operated according to the registered
VCS PD. Only renewable biomass (sugar cane bagasse and a
mix of sawdust and wood chips) is used in its burning
process.
The verifier identified one inconsistency on the
monitored parameters on the VCS PD, once the methodology
predicts that the parameter ƒNRB,y will be monitored and
this parameter was considered fixed on the VCS PD. To
comply with the methodology, this parameter was monitored
on this Monitoring Report, and this value is completely
detailed on Section B.4 of this report. The monitoring
data was kept according to monitoring plan described in
the VCS PD. The emission reductions due to the switching
non renewable biomass to renewable biomass resulted in
13,227 tCO2e during the monitoring period of January 1st,
2010 to July 31st, 2010.
Another inconsistency is related to the production
quantity monitoring. The registered VCS PD states that
the Pry is monitored in units of ceramic devices, and the
8

ceramic is monitoring this parameter in tons of products.
It were raised CARs related to this points, and the PP
corrected addressed them.

3.4 Accuracy of Emission Reduction Calculations
The approved CDM baseline and monitoring AMS-I.E (Switch
from Non – Renewable Biomass for Thermal Application by
the User) Version 01 from February 01st of 2008 onwards,
is applied to the project activity.
The Category AMS-I.E: Switch from Non – Renewable Biomass
for Thermal Application by the User (Version 01 from
February 01 of 2008 onwards) comprises small thermal
appliances that displace the use of non-renewable biomass
by introducing new renewable energy end-user
technologies. The project’s emissions from the combustion
of native wood are accounted in the same way as fossil
fuel combustion, once it is not renewable and emits CO2.

4 Verification conclusion
Sustainable Carbon Climate Solutions has commissioned the
BUREAU VERITAS Certification to carry out the 3rd periodic
verification of the project “Irmãos Fredi Ceramic Fuel
Switching Project”, with regard to the relevant
requirements for CDM project activities. This
verification covers the period from: January 01st, 2010 to
July 31st, 2010.
In the course of the verification, Corrective Action
Requests (CAR), Clarification Requests (CL), were raised
and successfully closed. There were no Forward Action
Requests (FAR) raised. The verification is based on the
monitoring report (Version 03, on September 14th, 2010),
the emission reduction calculation spreadsheet (Version
02 on July 30th, 2010), the monitoring plan as set out in
the registered VCS PD (Version 02 on January 14th, 2009),
and supporting documents made available to the BUREAU
VERITAS Certification by the project participant.
All information and documents made available to BUREAU
VERITAS by the PP are treated confidentially and were
only been used with objective to assess the data from the
monitored period and produce this Verification Report.
As a result of this verification, the DOE confirms that:
15
VCS 2007 Verification Report Template
• BUREAU VERITAS Certification confirms that the project
is implemented as planned and described in the Project
Design Document. The monitoring plan is in place and the
project is generating GHG emission reductions. The DOE
conclude, all operations of the project are implemented
and installed as planned and described as in the
registered VCS PD;
• The monitoring plan is in accordance with the applied
approved CDM methodology, i.e., AMS-I.E: Switch from non-
renewable biomass for thermal applications by the user -
Version 01, February 1st 2008 onwards;
BUREAU VERITAS Certification can confirm that the GHG
emission reductions are calculated without material
misstatements. The DOE conclude that in relation to the
project's GHG emissions, resulting GHG emission
reductions are consistent with the monitoring plan
contained in the VCS PD Version 02 on January 14th, 2009,
and VCS Monitoring Report Version 02, on July 30th, 2010,
so we consider correct.

Informações institucionais:
A Sustainable Carbon é especialista e líder na América Latina no desenvolvimento
de projetos de reduções de emissões e de soluções ligadas ao gerenciamento de
GasesdeEfeitoEstufa,GEEs.
Nossas soluções permitem que organizações e indivíduos conheçam e reduzam
seus impactos ambientais, possibilitando o equilíbrio das emissões e contribuindo
paraodesenvolvimentosustentáveldecomunidadesnomundotodo.
Também conectamos pessoas querendo fazer a diferença no mundo: empresários,
desenvolvedoresdeprojetos,empresas,ONGs,governos,associaçõeseentusiastas
detodoomundo.
Fazemos isso por meio de mecanismos técnicos e inovadores aprovados pelo
mercado de carbono que quantificam as reduções das emissões GEE.
Estabelecendo, assim, um preço sobre esse serviço ambiental e permitindo o
comérciointernacionaldestacommodityúnica:ocréditodecarbono.
Assim, aqueles que reduzem suas emissões recebem por isso e são capazes de
manter os projetos de carbono, que preservam ecossistemas ameaçados e geram
desenvolvimentonaregiãoondeestãolocalizados.
Somos especialistas no desenvolvimento de projetos de energia renovável, de
substituição de combustível, compostagem, projetos de biomassa e projetos
florestais.
Realizamos trabalhos em quase todos os ecossistemas brasileiros. Foi assim que
nos últimos anos a Sustainable Carbon entregou créditos de carbono da mais alta
qualidadeparacompensaçõesehojeéaempresalídernestesegmentonaAmérica
Latina.

A Sustainable Carbon desenvolve sistematização de coleta de dados que abrange todo o escopo de suas
atividades organizacionais, através de ferramentas corporativas e a difusão interna do conhecimento sobre as
emissõesdeGEE.Paraacoletainternadosdados,criamosumaplataformacomacessoatodososcolaboradores.
Este instrumento possibilita a coleta dos dados de acordo com a ocorrência da atividade e sua emissão,
facilitando o monitoramento dos indicadores, realização do inventário e também o processo de verificação por
terceiraparte.
Assim como os instrumentos corporativos, o conhecimento compartilhado junto aos colaboradores mostra-se
importante para a qualidade e transparência do inventário. Todos os colaboradores passam por orientações
quanto ao fornecimento dos dados necessários à elaboração do inventário, visando à difusão de conhecimento
sobreosinventáriosdeGEE.

Declaração de Verificação de inventário de emissões de gases de efeito estufa
Programa Brasileiro GHG Protocol
Esta Declaração de Verificação1 documenta que o Organismo de Verificação (OV) citado abaixo realizou as
atividades de verificação de acordo com as Especificações de Verificação do Programa Brasileiro GHG Protocol
e a norma ABNT NBR ISO 14064-3:2007.
Todos os campos são de preenchimento obrigatório.
Organismo de Verificação (OV) Organização Inventariante (OI)
Nome da OI: Sustainable Carbon - Projetos Ambientais
Nome do OV: VERIFIT LTDA
Ltda
Nome do verificador líder: Ricardo Lopes Nome do responsável pelo inventário: Aline Costa
E-mail: ricardo@verifit.com.br E-mail: aline.costa@sustainablecarbon.com
As emissões de gases de efeito estufa (GEE) informadas pela Organização Inventariante em seu inventário de
emissões, de 1º de janeiro até 31 de dezembro de 2023, são verificáveis e cumprem os requisitos do Programa
Brasileiro GHG Protocol, detalhados nas Especificações do Programa Brasileiro GHG Protocol de Contabilização,
Quantificação e Publicação de Inventários Corporativos de Emissões de Gases de Efeito Estufa (EPB).
Nível de Confiança
O Organismo de Verificação (OV) atribuiu o seguinte nível de confiança ao processo de verificação:
Verificação com nível de confiança razoável
“O inventário de gases de efeito estufa da organização inventariante para o ano de 2023 está materialmente
correto, é uma representação justa dos dados e informações de GEE e foi elaborado de acordo com as EPB.”
As limitações do processo de verificação foram: N/A
Verificação com nível de confiança limitado
“Não há indícios de que o inventário de gases de efeito estufa da organização inventariante para o ano de
[ano] não esteja materialmente correto, não seja uma representação justa dos dados e informações de GEE
e não tenha sido preparado de acordo com as EPB.”
As limitações do processo de verificação foram:

Summary:
Brief summary of the project activity
The project activity involves fuel switching from native firewood from caatinga biome to renewable
biomasses to generate thermal energy. Thus, GHG emission reduction is achieved.
The project applies the CDM approved methodology AMS-I.E – Switch from non-renewable biomass for
thermal applications by the user – version 01.0.
Scope of verification
Sustainable Carbon – Projetos Ambientais Ltda. has contracted ESPL to conduct the 3rd verification of
the project “GE Teobaldo Ceramic Fuel Switching Project” for the period from 01/03/2012 to
31/12/2018 (both days included).
The scope of the verification is to establish/verify that:
• the latest available MR template was used and correctly filled up;
• the project activity has been implemented and operated as per the registered PD and that all
physical features (technology, project equipment and monitoring and metering equipment) of
the project are in place;
• the monitoring report and other supporting documents provided are complete in accordance
with the latest applicable version of the completeness checklist for requests for issuance of
VCUs, verifiable, and in accordance with applicable requirements of the VCS Standard Version
4.4;
• the actual monitoring systems and procedures comply with the monitoring systems and
procedures described in the monitoring plan, the approved methodology including applicable
tool(s) and/or, where applicable, the approved standardized baseline;
• the data are recorded and stored as per the monitoring methodology including applicable
tool(s) and, where applicable, the standardized baseline.
In addition, ESPL has also been contracted to conduct the verification of SOCIALCARBON Standard of
the project activity at Point 3, with regard to its relevant requirements, for the same monitoring period.
Conclusion
Verification Report Template:
VCS Version 4.0, SOCIAL CARBON Standard
ESPL has performed the verification of the VCS project “GE Teobaldo Ceramic Fuel Switching Project”,
with VCS Project ID 586, for the monitoring period from 01/03/2012 to 31/12/2018 (both days
included).
During the present VCS verification, 02 CLs and 05 CARs were raised and successfully closed. For the
SOCIALCARBON verification, 01 CL was raised and successfully closed.
The verification team has confirmed the implementation of the project as per description in the VCS-
PD, the monitoring plan of the PD and the application of the monitoring methodology (AMS-I.E – version
01.0). In addition, it was confirmed that the monitoring system is in place and the emission reductions
are calculated without material misstatements.
The verified emission reductions amount are 30,324 tCO e in the above-mentioned monitoring period.
2
In addition, the verifier confirms that the project meets all relevant requirements established by the
SOCIALCARBON Standard and its report identifies the social economic and environmental impacts of
the project activity and presents the results obtained by meeting the “Brazilian Red Ceramic factories
Indicators” – version 1.1.

1 INTRODUCTION
1.1 Objective
Sustainable Carbon – Projetos Ambientais Ltda. has contracted ESPL to conduct the 3rd
verification of the project “GE Teobaldo Ceramic Fuel Switching Project” for the period from
01/03/2012 to 31/12/2018 (both days included) according to the requirements of the VCS
Standard Version 4.4.
1.2 Scope and Criteria
The scope of the verification is to establish/verify that:
- the project activity has been implemented and operated as per the registered PD and that all
physical features (technology, project equipment and monitoring) of the project are in place;
- the monitoring report and other supporting documents provided are complete in accordance
with the latest applicable version of the completeness checklist for requests for issuance of
VCUs, verifiable, and in accordance with applicable VCS Version 4 requirements;
- the actual monitoring systems and procedures comply with the monitoring systems and
procedures described in the monitoring plan, the approved methodology including applicable
tool(s) and/or, where applicable, the approved standardized baseline;
- the data are recorded and stored as per the monitoring methodology including applicable
tool(s) and, where applicable, the standardized baseline.
The verification of this monitoring period is based on the registered VCS-PD, MR and GHG
emission reduction calculation spreadsheet and supporting documents.
In addition, the scope of the SOCIALCARBON verification is to establish/verify that:
- to have an independent third-party assessment to evaluate the accuracy and consistency of
the SOCIALCARBON Standard – version 6.0 and “Brazilian Red Ceramic factories Indicators”
– version 1.1, for this specific point in time – Point 3; and
- to verify the consistency of these Indicators and their evolution since last evaluation, with the
established criteria to maintain the designation as SOCIALCARBON, following the
SOCIALCARBON Standard.
1.3 Level of Assurance
The verification of this monitoring period has achieved a reasonable level of assurance.
1.4 Summary Description of the Project
The project activity involves fuel switching from native wood for renewable biomasses to generate
thermal energy in GE Teobaldo Ceramic at the city of Paudalho, State of Pernambuco, Brazil.
Thus, GHG emission reduction is achieved.
Verification Report:
VCS Version 4.0, SOCIAL CARBON Standard
The project is listed at VCS and can be accessed by the following link:
https://registry.verra.org/app/projectDetail/VCS/586
Some of the characteristics of the project activity are described below:
- Project Proponents: GE Teobaldo Ceramic and Sustainable Carbon – Projetos Ambientais
Ltda.;
- Project Category: project (≤ 300,000 tCO2e/y);
- Methodology: AMS-I.E – Switch from non-renewable biomass for thermal applications by the
user – version 01.0;
- Start Date: 01/10/2008 – date on which GE Teobaldo Ceramic started using renewable
biomass as fuel;
- Crediting period: from 01/01/2009 to 31/12/2018 – first crediting period;
- Project Location: Estrada de Chã de Alegria, 1000 – City of Paudalho – State of Pernambuco
– Brazil – geographical coordinates are 7°55’41” S and 35°11’05” W;
- Project ownership: GE Teobaldo Ceramic is the project owner, as evidenced by its operation
license;
- Technical Data: 02 Hoffman kilns;
The project has several social and environmental components that are discussed in detail in
Section 4 below.
2 VERIFICATION PROCESS
2.1 Method and Criteria
The verification process is conducted as per internal CDM Quality Manual and in accordance with
criteria laid down by VCS. It includes the following steps:
- contract with PP for the scope and appointment of verification team and technical review
team;
- completeness check of Monitoring Report;
- desk review of Monitoring Report and corresponding ER sheet by verification team and
planning of onsite audit (including sampling approach to be applied);
- physical site inspection by verification team;
- follow up activities e.g., interviews;
- reporting and closure of findings (CARs/CLs/FARs) and preparation of draft verification report;
- independent technical review of the draft verification report and final/revised documentation
(e.g., Monitoring Report, corresponding ER sheet and evidence);
- reporting and closure of TR comments/findings (CARs/CLs/FARs) and final approval for the
decision made;

- issuance of final verification report to contracted PP (or authorized representatives).

- a review of the data and information presented to verify its completeness;

tool(s) and, where applicable, the applied standardized baseline, paying particular attention
to the frequency of measurements, the quality of metering equipment including calibration
requirements, and the quality assurance and quality control procedures;
- an evaluation of data management and the quality assurance and quality control system in
the context of their influence on the generation and reporting of emission reductions;
- supporting documents.

2.5 Resolution of Findings
The findings may be of following types: CAR – Corrective Action Request, CL – Clarification
Request and FAR – Forward Action Request.
During the present verification, 02 CLs and 05 CARs were raised and successfully closed.
For the SOCIALCARBON verification, 01 CL was raised and successfully closed.
The list of findings and their resolution are presented at Appendices IV and V of this report.
2.5.1 Forward Action Requests
No FAR was raised.
2.6 Eligibility for Validation Activities
ESPL holds the accreditation for validation of the sectoral scope.
3 VALIDATION FINDINGS
3.1 Participation under Other GHG Programs
The project activity has no participation under any other GHG program.
3.2 Methodology Deviations
The project activity complies with the applied methodology AMS-I.E – Switch from Non-Renewable
Biomass for Thermal Applications by the User – version 01.
No deviations were observed.
3.3 Project Description Deviations
According to information gathered during the site visit, the verification team has confirmed that
the project participants have been operating and monitoring the project activity, as per the
approved VCS-PD, during the present monitoring period. Nevertheless, it was verified that here
are two Hoffmann kilns, as a new one was constructed with exact same specifications of the
existing kiln. As the kilns are alternately used – i.e., when one is in operation, the other remains
inactive – there is no negative impact to the additionality of the project activity; in fact, there is a
positive impact to the additionality, as there was just an investment, without increase in the
production and revenues. So, this deviation does not impact the applicability of the methodology,
10
Verification Report:
VCS Version 4.0, SOCIAL CARBON Standard
additionality or the appropriateness of the baseline scenario, and the project remains in
conformance with the applied methodology.
Another deviation has been observed: the registered VCS-PD sets that the project proponent
(meaning the ceramic owner) would measure the quantity of renewable biomasses used
(parameter Q ). However, the parameter is monitored through the receipts and invoices of
renbiomass
biomass received by the ceramic company. This means that the amount of renewable biomass is
measured by the supplier and controlled by the ceramic owner by the receipts and invoices. This
approach considers that it is the responsibility of the provider to measure the amount of biomass,
since this information needs to be available in the sale invoice or receipt. As this information is
used for commercial purposes, it is considered that data from the suppliers are a reliable source
for this parameter. This correction was already implemented at previous monitoring period and
accepted by VCS. It is important to note that this deviation does not impact the applicability of
the methodology, additionality or the appropriateness of the baseline scenario, and the project
remains in conformance with the applied methodology.
3.4 Grouped Projects
The project activity is not a grouped project.
4 VERIFICATION FINDINGS
4.1 Project Implementation Status
The project activity involves fuel switching from native firewood from caatinga biome to renewable
biomasses to generate thermal energy. During this monitoring period, GE Teobaldo used algaroba
wood, native wood with sustainable management plan and wood residues as fuel.
As per the physical site assessment, observations, interviews and collected evidence, it was
possible to assess that, in general, the project activity has been implemented as described in the
VCS-PD, with all changes already approved in the previous crediting period.
There are no material discrepancies between the actual monitoring system, and the monitoring
plan set out in the project description and the applied methodology.
In addition, the GHG emission reductions generated by the project have not become included in
any emissions trading program or any other mechanism that includes GHG allowance trading.
Moreover, the project has not received nor sought any other form of environmental credit, and
neither has become eligible to do so since validation or previous verification. Finally, the project
has not participated nor been rejected under any other GHG programs since validation or
previous verification.
The main sustainable development contributions are:
a. use of clean and efficient technologies using renewable biomass as fuel;
b. pioneer initiative that encourages the development of new technologies throughout the
country;
c. use of renewable biomasses resulting in GHG emission reductions.
4.2 Safeguards
11
Verification Report:
VCS Version 4.0, SOCIAL CARBON Standard
4.2.1 No Net Harm
The burning of the biomasses emits particulate material and CO identified as negative impact.
2
This also occurs when using fossil fuel. Nevertheless, as they are renewable biomasses, they
have carbon neutral lifecycle.
The entrepreneur incorporates the ashes, resulting from the burning, into the clay mixture to
seal the kiln’s door.
Thus, the negative impacts caused by the project activity are being mitigated.
4.2.2 Local Stakeholder Consultation
The project participant has a good relationship with local community and has open channels (i.e.,
telephone, e-mail and/or personally) in order to receive comments from local stakeholders. Thus,
the project activity has a local stakeholder consultation access in place and working.
No comments/complaints have been observed during the monitoring period.
4.2.3 AFOLU-Specific Safeguards
No applicable as it is a non-AFOLU project.
4.3 Accuracy of GHG Emission Reduction and Removal Calculations
- the approved CDM methodology AMS-I.E – Switch from non-renewable biomass for thermal
applications by the user – version 01 is applied to the project activity. GHG emission
reductions are calculated as baseline emissions;
- no project emissions are considered for the project activity, in accordance with the applied
methodology;
- no leakage is considered for this kind of project, in accordance with the applied methodology
and “General guidance on leakage in biomass project activities”, as all possible sources of
leakage are demonstrated at the MR as:
o there is no transference of equipment;
o the quantity of biomasses to provide thermal energy for the project activity represents a
non-representative quantity of these kind of biomasses, which avoids the possibility of
leakage;
o the project is expected to decrease the use of non-renewable biomass by similar users,
especially due to the incentive of carbon credits;
- all methods and formulas used in the calculations of emission reductions have been followed
in accordance with the applied methodology and monitoring plan of the validated at VCS-PD;
- all monitored data is traceable.
Fixed parameters – the values in the MR and used in corresponding emission reduction
calculations spreadsheet are consistent with the registered VPA-DD:

4.6 SOCIALCARBON Results
The SocialCarbon indicators have been monitored in a correct and conservative way. Their

participants. The procedures to gather all data, analysing, recording, reporting and compilation
have been performed by personnel trained for the tasks (in GE Teobaldo Ceramic and Sustainable
Carbon).
All above information could be confirmed by the performed interviews, site visit and records
presented to the verification team.
Therefore, the verification team has concluded that the monitoring of the SocialCarbon indicators
is in accordance with the registered monitoring plan.

4.6.4 Natural Resource
Indicator Environmental management
Situation The ceramic factory shows some initiatives regarding environmental
management:
- Waste: the factory gives an adequate destination to residues since the
unburned residues are returned into the clay mixture and reused in the
production process, while the ashes generated by GE Teobaldo’s
production are used to seal the kiln’s doors. In addition, the burned
residues are donated to the community;
- Energy: the use of translucent tiles and LED lamps enhance energy saving;
- Air: there were annual reports that analysed and quantified the
atmospheric emissions of greenhouse gases, aiming to control and reduce
the emissions;
- - Water: the ceramic uses a rainwater collection system.

4.6.5 Biodiversity/Technology Resource
Indicator Technological advances to improve efficiency of the production process
Situation The factory meets the following aspects:
- Brick drying methods: the drying occurs naturally in a covered place,
without reusing heat from kilns. However, on rainy days the factory uses
mechanic fans to help the drying process.
- Efficiency of kilns: GE Teobaldo ceramic uses two Hoffman Kilns active
interspersed. Thus, when one works, the other remains inactive, this type
of kiln are considered a high-efficiency kiln;
- Kiln temperature control: the ceramic factories control the temperature of
the kilns in the firing process using an electronic thermometer on the kiln;
- Kiln feeds: the factory feeds the kiln manually;
- Production: the factory controls and records production at different stages;
and
- Infrastructure: improvements such as the renovation of the refectory.

6 VERIFICATION OPINION
ESPL, contracted by Sustainable Carbon – Projetos Ambientais Ltda., has performed the
independent verification of the emission reductions of the VCS project “GE Teobaldo Ceramic
Fuel Switching Project”, with VCS Project ID 586, for the monitoring period 01/03/2012 to
31/12/2018 (both days included).
Sustainable Carbon – Projetos Ambientais Ltda. is responsible for the collection of data in
accordance with the monitoring plan and the reporting of GHG emissions reductions from the
project activity.
The GHG statement is the responsibility of the project proponent, whether the project conforms
with the verification criteria for projects set out in VCS Version 4.
ESPL commenced the verification based on the baseline and monitoring methodology AMS-I.E –
version 01, the monitoring plan contained in the registered VCS-PD and draft Monitoring Report.
ESPL’s verification approach is based on the understanding of the risks associated with reporting
of GHG emission data and the controls in place to mitigate these. ESPL planned and performed
the verification by obtaining evidence and other information and explanations that ESPL
considered necessary to give reasonable assurance that reported GHG emission reductions are
fairly stated.
The verification team confirms that:
• the project activity was found completely implemented as per the description given in the
registered VCS-PD; and
• the actual operation conforms to the description in the VCS-PD.
Thus, the GHG emissions reductions reported for the project activity for the period from
01/03/2012 to 31/12/2018 (including both days) are fairly stated in the final version of the
34
Verification Report:
VCS Version 4.0, SOCIAL CARBON Standard
Monitoring Report. The GHG emission reductions were calculated correctly based on the
approved baseline and monitoring methodology AMS-I.E – version 01 and the monitoring plan
contained in the VCS-PD.
Therefore, ESPL can give its verification opinion about the emission reductions from the VCS
project “GE Teobaldo Ceramic Fuel Switching Project”.
In addition, as a result of the SOCIALCARBON verification, the verifier confirms that:
• the indicators and reports are in accordance with SOCIALCARBON Standard and “Brazilian
Red Ceramic factories Indicators” – version 1.1;
• the project is being periodically monitored and Point Zero is being used as the baseline;
• although Social and Carbon resources had a decrease in the performance on this period in
comparison to the previous one, there are perspectives of improvement and the efforts to
seek the improvement are being done;
• the report has been validated by an accredited entity, a physical site visit has been carried out
and this verification report has been issued.
In addition, the verifier confirms that the indicators for “Brazilian Red Ceramic factories
Indicators” – version 1.1 are accurate and the calculations are correct. The SOCIALCARBON
template and SOCIALCARBON Guidelines were correctly used.
ESPL herewith confirms that the project is in line with all applicable criteria for the verification.
This verification was conducted in accordance with requirements of ISO 14064-3:2019.
The characterization of the Point 3 for the project as “SATISFACTORY”, as described in the
referenced documents, is accurate.

Description of CAR
In Section 4.2 of the MR:
a. parameter Origin of renewable biomass: the monitoring frequency of the parameter is not in

NRB,y

Project participant response Date: 15/03/2023
a. parameter Origin of renewable biomass: the monitoring frequency of the parameter was
corrected in accordance with the registered monitoring plan;
b. parameter Renewable biomass surplus: According to the “General guidance on leakage in
biomass project activities”1, the project participant shall evaluate ex ante if there is a biomass
surplus in the region of the project activity, which is not utilized. If it is demonstrated (e.g.,
using published literature, official reports, surveys etc.) at the beginning of each crediting
period that the quantity of available biomass in the region, is at least 25% larger than the
quantity of biomass that is utilized including the project activity, then this source of leakage
can be neglected; otherwise, this leakage shall be estimated and deducted from the emission
reductions. During the monitoring period, Sustainable Carbon analysed if the quantity of
available biomass in the region (which was determined ex ante), was at least 25% larger than
the quantity of biomass that was utilized by the project activity, as it was and as can be seen
in Section 5.3 Leakage. This section contains an assessment of the total of Algaroba Wood,
Native wood with sustainable forest management plan and Wood Residues available in the
region, considering other economic uses of biomass. And, according to the “General guidance
on leakage in biomass project activities”, it has been demonstrated that there is a biomass
surplus (Algaroba Wood, Native wood with sustainable forest management plan and Wood
Residues) greater than 25%2 including the project activities consumption, and therefore this
source of leakage can be neglected.
c. parameter fNRB,y: The calculation of fNRB was rectified and now the monitoring comprehends
a yearly analysis. These annually values were calculated in the VCS-MR Calculation GE
Teobaldo Spreadsheet Version 02 - “FNRB,y” tab. The corresponding value for each year was
used for the calculation of Bey - “3th period - VCUs Calculations” Tab. Also, the values were
reported in the section 4.2 “Data and Parameters Monitored” in the VCS+SocialCarbon
Monitoring Report Version 02. The values for the fNRB parameter calculated for the years

• 2014= 92.20%
• 2015= 92.10%
• 2016= 92.10%
• 2017= 92.10%
• 2018= 90.90%

seus impactos ambientais, possibilitando o equilíbrio das emissões e contribuindo

empresas foram consideradas desde janeiro de 2024, o que contribuiu significativamente para o aumento
observado. Além disso, assim como no ano anterior, as viagens a negócios se mantiveram como a principal fonte
de emissões da organização, intensificadas pelo grande número de deslocamentos realizados ao longo do ano,
especialmenteapósaintegraçãodasequipeseaampliaçãodasatividadesoperacionais.

A administração do condomínio é responsável pelo controle da energia elétrica adquirida para a área comum do
prédio, recarga de extintores e também geradores. Essas emissões foram excluídas do inventário de 2024 por
dificuldadenacoletadedadoseporseremirrelevantesnaproporçãodaáreadeusodaSustainableCarbon.Para
o ano de 2024, também foram reportadas emissões de energia elétrica no Escopo 3, correspondentes ao
consumo dos funcionários durante o expediente de trabalho remoto. Em relação à manutenção de sistemas de
climatização,houveregistroderecargadegásrefrigerantenosaparelhosdear-condicionado,porémarecargafoi
realizada com R-22. Embora o R-22 seja reconhecido como um gás de efeito estufa (GEE) com alto potencial de
aquecimento global (PAG), ele não é incluído em inventários de GEE no âmbito do Protocolo de Kyoto, pois está
sujeito ao controle do Protocolo de Montreal, que tem como objetivo a proteção da camada de ozônio. Dessa
forma,essaemissãonãofoiconsideradanoescopodopresenteinventário.

A Sustainable Carbon realiza a sistematização da coleta de dados que abrange todo o escopo de suas atividades
organizacionais por meio de planilhas estruturadas, enviadas aos colaboradores para preenchimento. Essa
abordagem é complementada pela difusão interna de conhecimento sobre emissões de GEE, promovendo o
entendimento e o comprometimento com a qualidade das informações fornecidas. As planilhas são elaboradas
de forma a permitir que os dados sejam coletados conforme a ocorrência das atividades, o que facilita a
consolidação do inventário e o processo de verificação por terceira parte. Além disso, os colaboradores recebem
orientações específicas sobre o preenchimento correto das informações, reforçando a importância da sua
participaçãoepromovendomaiorclarezaetransparêncianaelaboraçãodoinventáriodeGEE.

VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
Summary:
 A brief description of the verification and the project
Verification: Sustainable Carbon – Projetos Ambientais Ltda has commissioned Carbon Check
(Pty) Ltd., to carry out the 3rd verification of the project “Reunidas Ceramic Switching Non-
Renewable Biomass Project”, with regards to the relevant requirements of VCS Standard Version
3.4 (dated 08/10/2013).
Project: The project envisages fuel Switch to Renewable Biomass for Thermal Use at Reunidas
Ceramics, the Project). The Project claims emission reductions due to switching non-renewable to
renewable biomass for end user thermal energy generation. The verification covers the period
from 01/11/2010 – 31/07/2013 (both days inclusive).
 The purpose and scope of verification
Purpose: Verification is the periodic independent review and ex post determination of both
quantitative and qualitative information by a Validation and Verification Body (VVB) of the
monitored reductions in GHG emissions that have occurred as a result of the VCS project activity
during a defined monitoring period.
The purpose of verification is to review the monitoring results and verify that the project activity is
implemented according to the monitoring plan and monitoring data, and used to confirm the
reductions in anthropogenic emissions by sources, is sufficient, definitive and presented in a
concise and transparent manner. Carbon Check’s objective is to perform a thorough, independent
assessment of the registered project activities.
In particular the, monitoring plan, monitoring report and the project’s compliance are verified
against the relevant criteria and guidance documents provided by VCS which, included the
following: VCS Program Guide (v3.5, dated 08/10/2013), VCS Standard (v3.4, dated 08/10/2013),
Program Definitions (v3.5, dated 08/10/2013), Registration & Issuance Process (v3.5, dated
08/10/2013) and in line with the VCS Validation and Verification Manual (v 3.1, dated 08/10/2013)
applicable at the time in order to confirm that the project has been implemented in accordance
with the previously validated VCS PD and conservative assumptions, as documented. And also to
confirm whether the monitoring plan is in compliance with the validated VCS PD and approved
monitoring methodology.
Scope:
 To verify the project implementation and operation with respect to the validated VCS PD
 To verify the implemented monitoring plan with the validated VCS PD and applied baseline and
monitoring methodology.
 To verify that the actual monitoring systems and procedures are in compliance with the
monitoring systems and procedures described in the monitoring plan.
 To evaluate the GHG emission reduction data and express a conclusion with a reasonable
level of assurance about whether the reported GHG emission reduction data is free from
material misstatement.
 To verify that reported GHG emission data is sufficiently supported by evidence.
The verification shall ensure that the reported emission reductions are complete and accurate in
order to be certified.
v3.3 2
VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
 The method and criteria used for verification
The verification consists of the following three phases:
1. Completeness check and desk review of the validation report, monitoring plan, monitoring
report, monitoring methodology, VCS PD, applicable tools in particular attention to the
frequency of measurements, quality of metering equipment’s including calibration
requirements, QA/QC procedures and other relevant documents;
2. On-site visit (including follow-up interviews with project stakeholders, when deemed
necessary). The on-site assignment includes the following;
 An assignment of implementation and operation of project activity with respect to validated
VCS PD;
 Review of information flows for generating, aggregating and reporting the monitoring
parameters;
 Interview with relevant personals to determine whether the operational and data collection
procedures are implemented and in accordance with monitoring plan of the validated VCS
PD;
 Cross check of information and data provided in the monitoring report with plant logbooks,
inventories, purchase records or similar data sources;
 Check of monitoring equipment’s, calibration frequency and monitoring practice in-line with
methodology and validated VCS PD;
 Review of assumptions made in calculating the emission reduction;
 Implementation of QA/QC procedure in-line with the validated VCS PD and methodology
requirement.
3. Resolution of outstanding issues and the issuance of the final Verification Report and VCS
Verification Representation.
 The number of findings raised during verification
During the course of verification 12 Corrective Action Requests (CARs) and 11 Clarification
Requests (CLs) were raised.
 Any uncertainties associated with the verification
The Emission Reductions have been correctly calculated. A complete set of data for the specified
monitoring period was available and verified while undertaking site visit. The verification team has
detected no further uncertainties or quality restriction. The MR /03/, emissions reduction
calculations /06/ and the approved VCS PD /B07/ along with the supporting documents provided
are considered to be in line with the VCS v.3 requirements.
