1 INTRODUCTION
Sustainable Carbon – Projetos Ambientais Ltda. has commissioned the VVB, Carbon Check (Pty)
Ltd. to perform an independent verification of the VCS Project Activity “Reunidas Ceramic
Switching Non-Renewable Biomass Project” in the state of Tocantins of Brazil (hereafter referred
to as “project activity”). This report summarises the findings of the verification of the project,
performed on the basis of the VCS Program Guide (v3.5, dated 08/10/2013), VCS Standard
(v3.4, dated 08/10/2013), Program Definitions (v3.5, dated 08/10/2013), Registration & Issuance
Process (v3.5, dated 08/10/2013) and VCS Validation and Verification Manual (v 3.1, dated
08/10/2013). Verification is required for all VCS project activities intending to confirm their
achieved emission reductions and proceed with request for issuance of VCUs. This report
contains the findings and resolutions from the verification and a certification statement for the
verified emission reductions.
1.1 Objective
Verification is the periodic independent review and ex post determination of both quantitative and
qualitative information by a Validation and Verification Body (VVB) of the monitored reductions in
GHG emissions that have occurred as a result of the VCS project activity during a defined
monitoring period.
The purpose of verification is to review the monitoring results and verify that the monitoring
methodology was implemented according to the monitoring plan and monitoring data, and used to
confirm the reductions in anthropogenic emissions by sources, is sufficient, definitive and
presented in a concise and transparent manner. Carbon Check’s objective is to perform a
thorough, independent assessment of the registered projects activities.
In particular the, monitoring plan, monitoring report and the project’s compliance are verified
against the relevant criteria and guidance documents provided by VCS which, included the
following: VCS Program Guide (v3.5, dated 08/10/2013), VCS Standard (v3.4, dated 08/10/2013),
Program Definitions (v3.5, dated 08/10/2013), Registration & Issuance Process (v3.5, dated
08/10/2013) and VCS Validation and Verification Manual (v 3.1, dated 08/10/2013). This allows
for the confirmation that the project has been implemented in accordance with the previously
validated VCS PD and conservative assumptions, as documented. And also if the monitoring plan
is in compliance with the validated VCS PD (version 05 dated 04/05/2009) and approved
monitoring methodology.
1.2 Scope and Criteria
The verification of this project is based on the registered PD version 05 /B07/, Monitoring Report
of this monitoring period /03/, emission reduction calculation spread sheet /06/, supporting
documents made available to the verifier and information collected through performing interviews
and during the on-site assessment. Furthermore publicly available information was considered as
far as available and required.
Carbon Check has employed a risk-based approach in the verification, focusing on the
identification of significant risks and reliability of project monitoring and generation of emission
reductions.
The verification is carried out on the basis of the following requirements, applicable for this project
activity:
 VCS Program Guide (v3.5, dated 08/10/2013)

The main scopes of the verification are:
 To verify the project implementation and operation with respect to the validated VCS PD
 To verify the implemented monitoring plan with the validated VCS PD and applied baseline
and monitoring methodology.
 To verify that the actual monitoring systems and procedures are in compliance with the
monitoring systems and procedures described in the monitoring plan.
 To evaluate the GHG emission reduction data and express a conclusion with a reasonable
level of assurance about whether the reported GHG emission reduction data is free from
material misstatement.
 To verify that reported GHG emission data is sufficiently supported by evidence.
 The verification shall ensure that the reported emission reductions are complete and
accurate in order to be certified.
1.3 Level of Assurance
In line with VCS version 3 requirements, a reasonable level of assurance is defined for the
verification of the project.
This implies that, based on the process and procedures conducted, Carbon Check confirms that
the GHG assertion in the monitoring report:
 Is materially correct and is a fair representation of the GHG data and information, and
 Is prepared in accordance with VCS version 3 requirements, the validated VCS PD and the
approved methodology for information pertaining to GHG quantification, monitoring and
reporting.
Assessment on the consideration of materiality in verification:
During course of verification, the verification team has considered and applied concept of
materiality as per the VVM and VCS standard. Materiality assessment corresponds to a
reasonable level of assurance and thus verification team during the course of verification,
requires PP to comply and reach to point where emission reductions are free from material errors,
omissions or misstatements. Verification team considered the concept of materiality in detection
of errors, omissions or misstatements in emission reductions claimed by project participants in the
monitoring report /03/ and associated emission reduction spread sheet /06/. The concept of
materiality has been considered in planning (identification of materiality threshold, understanding
the project and different emission sources for BE, PE and their % contribution in emission
reduction, risk assessment and preparation of verification and sampling plan for execution of the
verification i.e. for desk review and OSV check), execution (apply verification and sampling plan
during desk review and onsite visit data check) and reporting (refer findings in this report)

1.4 Summary Description of the Project
Reunidas Ceramic is a ceramic company located in the municipality of Cristalândia in the state of
Tocantins, northern region of Brazil, at Av. Tocantins, s/n, St. Ceramica, Cristalandia – Tocantins,
Brazil. Zip code – 77.710-000, Latitude: 10˚ 36’11.4” S Longitude: 49˚ 11’54.6” W, checked and
confirmed by the verifier during OSV and crossed checked through web-research /B06-4/ /B07/.
During the onsite visit carried out on 27/08/2013 and 29/08/2013 it was possible to assess that
Reunidas Ceramic produces ceramic devices in six “Round” kiln, which were described in the
VCS PD version 05, and also in one new Round kiln that were not mentioned in the VCS PD. It
was confirmed through interviews onsite.
The Project consists of a fuel switch in the ceramic facility, with the substitution of unsustainable
native firewood to rick husk, for thermal energy generation used on the ceramic firing process for
the production of tiles and bricks, destined mainly for the local market. The project activity
involves fuel switching from native firewood from Cerrado biome to rick husk originated from rice
mills of the region used to feed the ceramic kilns; thus GHG emission reduction is achieved.
The GHG reduction for the considered monitoring period is 24,982 tCO e /06/.

2.1 Method and Criteria
The method and criteria used for verification consists of the following three phases:
v3.3 10
VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
1. Completeness check and desk review of the validation report, monitoring plan, monitoring
report, monitoring methodology, VCS PD, applicable tools in particular attention to the
frequency of measurements, quality of metering equipment’s including calibration
requirements, QA/QC procedures and other relevant documents;
2. On-site visit (including follow-up interviews with project stakeholders, when deemed
necessary). The on-site assignment includes the following;
 An assignment of implementation and operation of project activity with respect to
validated VCS PD;
 Review of information flows for generating, aggregating and reporting the monitoring
parameters;
 Interview with relevant personals to determine whether the operational and data
collection procedures are implemented and in accordance with monitoring plan of the
validated VCS PD;
 Cross check of information and data provided in the monitoring report with plant
logbooks, inventories, purchase records or similar data sources;
 Check of monitoring equipment’s, calibration frequency and monitoring practice in-line
with methodology and validated VCS PD;
 Review of assumptions made in calculating the emission reduction;
 Implementation of QA/QC procedure in-line with the validated VCS PD and
methodology requirement.
3. Resolution of outstanding issues and the issuance of the final Verification report and if
applicable the VCS Verification and Issuance Deed of Representation.

2.4 Site Inspections
Site inspection was carried out to verify that the project is implemented in accordance with the
applicable criteria. On-site assessment is necessary to check the monitoring data with respect to
accuracy to ensure the calculation of emission reductions. The main tasks carried out during site
inspection are:
 The on-site assessment included an investigation of whether all relevant equipment is
installed and works as anticipated.
 The operating staff was interviewed and observed in order to check the risks of inappropriate
operation and data collection procedures.
 Information processes for generating; aggregating and reporting the selected monitored
parameters were reviewed.
 The duly calibration/testing of all metering equipment was checked.
 The monitoring processes, routines and documentations were audited to check their proper
application.
 The monitoring data were checked completely.
2.5 Resolution of Findings
This section summarises the findings from the verification of the voluntary emission reductions
reported for the project, with a monitoring period from 01/11/2010 to 31/07/2013. In this section
the findings from the document review, site visit, assessments and interviews are provided.
Findings during the verification can be interpreted as a non-compliance with CDM criteria or a risk
to the compliance.
 Corrective action requests (CARs) are raised, The VVB has identified a material
discrepancy or non-conformance that the project proponent must address.
 Requests for clarification (CLs) are raised, Project reporting lacks transparency and
further information is needed to determine if a material discrepancy is present.
 A forward action request (FAR) is raised during verification to highlight issues related
to project implementation/monitoring that require review during the subsequent
verification of the project activity. FARs shall not relate to the VCS requirements for
issuance

4 VERIFICATION FINDINGS
4.1 Project Implementation Status
The Project consists of a fuel switch in the ceramic facility, with the substitution of unsustainable
native firewood to rice husk in Reunidas ceramic, which produces structural ceramic devices like
bricks. The project activity involves fuel switching from native firewood from Cerrado biome to rice
husk originated from rice mills of the region used to feed the ceramic kilns; thus GHG emission
reduction is achieved.
v3.3 15
VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
The GHG reduction for the considered monitoring period is 24,982 tCO e /06/.
2
The Project is owned by Cerâmica Reunidas Ltda. and developed by Sustainable Carbon –
Projetos Ambientais Ltda. (both Project Proponents) and is located at Av. Tocantins, s/n, St.
Ceramica, Cristalandia – Tocantins, Brazil. Zip code – 77.710-000. Latitude: 10˚ 36’11.4” S
Longitude: 49˚ 11’54.6 W”, checked and confirmed by the verification team during OSV and
crossed checked through web-research /B06-4/ /B07/.
The rated installed capacity and technical specification of the kiln has been checked during the
OSV by means of physical verification and through document review /19/ /20/ /21/.
The date of initiating project activities occurred in October, 2007. Project validation has been
concluded on 5th May 2009 as verified from the validation report /B07/ of the project available on
VCS website.
The implemented project activity is a fuel switch to renewable biomass, as verified during the
OSV and document review /B07/, /03/, /09/, /19/. In the pre-project scenario, the plant used to
consume about 12,000 m3 of native wood from Cerrado biome per year to feed the kilns and
maintain a temperature of 900˚C for 48 hours producing around 400,000 ceramic devices per
month. During the verified monitoring period, the monthly average production of the ceramic
pieces was 368,782, which prove that the project activity has been developed according to the
registered VCS PD.
As verified during the OSV and documents reviewed /B07/ /03/ /19/ /21/, for the corresponding
monitoring period a new round kiln was constructed and which started operation in June, 2013.
As the ceramic company always used exclusively renewable biomass in the new kiln, during the
current monitoring period the additionality assessment was performed in order to include the
production provided by these new kilns. The additionality assessment was verified based on
assessing the comparison made between the cost of energy generated on the baseline scenario
of Reunidas Ceramic and at the current monitoring period, detailed in section 2.1 of VCS MR and
supported in the excel sheet calculations /06/. The verification team was able to verify that all
assumptions have been used in a proper manner, thus it is consistent to affirm that the project is
still additional and that the emission reductions calculated for this monitoring period is
conservative in comparison with the one predicted in the baseline emission of the approved PD
version 05 in which emission reduction calculation was estimated based on 6 round kilns only.
As defined in the approved VCS PD /B07/ and verified during the OSV interview /II/, /III/, /IV/, the
biomass utilised in the project activity (also during the monitoring period) falls under the category
of renewable biomass (EB 23 Annex 18). The verification team, based on the OSV verification
confirms that the biomass procured during the monitoring period from each vendor is within the
distance of 200 km and thus leakage on the account of biomass transportation is not applicable
for the project activity, checked and confirmed to be correct by the verification team. Review of
approved VCS PD /B07/, also confirms that there is a surplus of biomass in the region of
approximately a 100-mile radius from the project site. The surplus abundance of the biomass is
demonstrated at the validation stage and as per requirement of Attachment C to Appendix B it is
fixed ex-ante and would remain valid for the entire crediting period. Hence leakage emission on
account of competitive use of biomass is not applicable to the project activity, checked and
confirmed to be correct by the verification team.
As stated in the VCS MR /03/ the parameter ƒ has been monitored by survey methods based
NRB.y
in national and international articles available in the country, which is in line with the requirements
of the applied methodology and the register PD. In order to determine the fraction of non-
renewable biomass, the PP considered the area of Cerrado Biome with sustainable use (1.9%)
/B08/ /06/ and also the amount of non-renewable wood saved under similar project activities in
the same Cerrado Biome (0.0245%) /B09/ /06/ in order to determine a value of 98.07%. The
verification team could check that a conservative criteria has been used for the calculation of the

parameter ƒ and could confirm that a verifiable source was used for the assumptions when
NRB.y
calculating the value of 98.07%.
As stated on the VCS MR /03/, a deviation from the monitoring plan regarding the measurement
of the amount of renewable biomasses used (Q ) was observed. The registered VCS PD
renbiomass
version 05 /B07/ establishes that the project proponent (ceramic owner) would measure the
amount of renewable biomass used (Q ). However, during the entire monitored period, this
renbiomass
parameter was monitored through all the receipts and invoices of biomass received by the
ceramic industry. This means that the amount of renewable biomass is measured by each
provider and controlled by the ceramic owner by storing receipts and invoices. The verification
team checked the receipts and invoices of the biomass and considered the deviation on current
monitoring period as compared to registered VCS PD is acceptable and reliable.
Verification team based on the above assessment confirms the following:
 Project has been implemented as per the approved VCS PD and there is no deviation.
 Monitoring plan has been implemented as stated in the approved VCS PD (except above
assessed deviations).
 Verification team confirms the completeness of monitoring.
 There are no remaining issues from previous validation.
 The project activity is not registered under any other GHG program.
 The Project activity has no intends to generate any other form of GHG-related
environmental credit for GHG emission reductions or removals claimed under the VCS
Program.
4.2 Accuracy of GHG Emission Reduction and Removal Calculations
Review of MR /03/ and approved VCS PD /B07/ reveals that emission reductions have been
calculated in accordance with the applied methodology AMS-I.E. “Switch from non-renewable
biomass for thermal application by the user” (Version 1.0) /B02/. A linked spread-sheet has been
provided for calculations of VCUs /06/. No significant reporting risks have been identified for the
data reported. Emission reduction calculations for the verification period were reviewed to ensure
accuracy in the formulas applied and the raw data and default factors used as inputs. The formulas
were tested and found to be consistent with the calculations described in AMS-I.E. (Version 1.0)
/B02/ and the validated VCS PD /B07/.

calculation was considered to be in accordance with the parameters fixed ex ante in the
registered VCS PD /B07/.
All plant data and historical data cross checked during OSV and found to be appropriate.
The applied methodology does not include any source of project emissions.
No leakage emissions are attributed to the project activity during the verification period as all kilns
being used prior to the project activity have not been sold and the energy generating equipment
being used under the project activity have not been transferred from outside the boundary to the
project activity. The same was verified and confirmed by the verification team during the site visit.
Moreover, leakage emissions due to transportation of biomass to the project site have not been
considered. The only possible source of leakage of the present project activity is the competing
use of biomass residues, as rice husk but it was confirmed through survey /03/ /06/ conducted by
the PP to assess surplus biomass in the region. All possible sources of leakage assessed in the
MR /03/ and the verification confirmed through ex post surveys and OSV that the project activity
does not encompass any type of leakage in this monitoring period.
4.3 Quality of Evidence to Determine GHG Emission Reductions and Removals
The quality of supporting evidences submitted to the DOE for verification is adequate and found
to be verifiable.
The amount of renewable biomass employed was checked by accessing all the receipts and
invoices of the biomass received by the ceramic /09.a/ and other supporting documents such as
excel sheets with the registry of the daily consumption /09.b/ was assessed in order to cross
check values used in the emissions reduction calculations.
The production of ceramic units per month was checked by assessing the internal control sheets
/10.a/ used by Reunidas Ceramic, which are organized per month and daily filled according to
data based on ceramic units burned in the kilns, also the excel sheets /10.b/ containing the
consolidated data of production was assessed.
The verification team for the records and future reference also obtained sample copies for the
amount of renewable biomass consumed and amount of ceramic units produced.
v3.3 18
VERIFICATION REPORT
: VCS Version 3
The Emission Reductions have been correctly calculated. A complete set of data for the specified
monitoring period was available and verified while undertaking the site visit. The verification team
has reviewed the above-mentioned evidences and considered that the calculations are in line
with VCS requirements.
Management and Operational System
The verification team based on the review of MR /03/, approved VCS PD /B07/ and onsite
interviews/observations confirms that Reunidas has adequate management and operational
systems in place with respect to monitoring and reporting. Roles and responsibilities related to
the monitoring of the project are adequately defined.
Representative of Sustainable Carbon – Projetos Ambientais Ltda and Reunidas, who are
responsible for the monitoring and reporting of this VCS project were interviewed /I/,/II/,/III/, /IV/,
/V/ during the OSV and found to be competent, qualified and familiar with the monitoring/reporting
requirement. Furthermore it was noted that Sustainable Carbon is responsible for calculating the
emission reductions and writing the monitoring report.
It was confirmed during the site visit that the operational structure is in place as stated in
monitoring plan /B07/.
Sustainable Carbon in consultation with Reunidas manages the entire data collection, recording
and archiving of information relating to the biomass project. Sustainable Carbon archives all
records at its office in Sao Paulo. Sustainable Carbon is also responsible for training and periodic
performance review of the project and the same has been confirmed through OSV and document
review /15/ /16/.
Data collection, measurement, calibration, recording and archiving was found to be carried out as
per the monitoring plan stated in validated VCS PD /B07/.
4.4 Non-Permanence Risk Analysis
Not applicable
5 VERIFICATION CONCLUSION
Carbon Check has performed the verification of the project activity “Reunidas Ceramic Switching
Non-Renewable Biomass Project” in Brazil, with regards to the relevant requirements for VCS
project activities.
Review of validated VCS PD /B07/ and subsequent follow-up interviews have provided Carbon
Check with sufficient evidence to determine the fulfilment of the stated criteria. The Project has
been implemented in accordance with the project description and subsequently validated
variations.
The project activity results in the reduction of carbon dioxide (CO ) emissions that are real,
2
measurable and give long-term benefits to the mitigation of climate change. The total emission
reductions from the Project for the monitoring period 01/11/2010 to 31/07/2013 are 24,982 tCO e
2
/06/.
In Carbon Check’s opinion, the GHG emission reduction stated in the monitoring report for the
monitoring period 01/11/2010 to 31/07/2013 are fairly stated. The GHG emission reductions are
calculated correctly on the basis of the approved monitoring methodology AMS-I.E. “Switch from
Non-Renewable Biomass for Thermal Application by the User” (Version 1.0) /B02/.

Inventário de emissões de gases de efeito estufa
Ano inventariado: 2014
Inventário 2014 - Sustainable Carbon
SUSTAINABLE CARBON - PROJETOS AMBIENTAIS LTDA.
Nome fantasia: Sustainable Carbon
CNPJ: 09.510.073/0001-06
Setor econômico: Atividades profissionais, científicas e técnicas
Subsetor: Atividades profissionais, científicas e técnicas
Endereço: R DOUTOR BACELAR - 368 - ANDAR 02 Conjunto 23 - Vila Clementino - São
Paulo - SP - 04.026-001
Responsável pela publicação do inventário: Dênis Gonçalves
(denis@sustainablecarbon.com)
Informações institucionais
A Sustainable Carbon é especialista e líder na América Latina no desenvolvimento de
projetos de reduções de emissões e de soluções ligadas ao gerenciamento de Gases de
Efeito Estufa, GEEs.
Nossas soluções permitem que organizações e indivíduos conheçam e reduzam seus
impactos ambientais, possibilitando o equilíbrio das emissões e contribuindo para o
desenvolvimento sustentável de comunidades no mundo todo.
Também conectamos pessoas querendo fazer a diferença no mundo: empresários,
desenvolvedores de projetos, empresas, ONGs, governos, associações e entusiastas de
todo o mundo.
Fazemos isso por meio de mecanismos técnicos e inovadores aprovados pelo mercado de
carbono que quantificam as reduções das emissões GEE. Estabelecendo, assim, um preço
sobre esse serviço ambiental e permitindo o comércio internacional desta commodity
única: o crédito de carbono.
Assim, aqueles que reduzem suas emissões recebem por isso e são capazes de manter os
projetos de carbono, que preservam ecossistemas ameaçados e geram desenvolvimento
na região onde estão localizados.
Somos especialistas no desenvolvimento de projetos de energia renovável, de
substituição de combustível, compostagem, projetos de biomassa e projetos florestais.
Realizamos trabalhos em quase todos os ecossistemas brasileiros. Foi assim que nos
últimos anos a Sustainable Carbon entregou créditos de carbono da mais alta qualidade
para compensações e hoje é a empresa líder neste segmento na América Latina.
Veja abaixo nossas conquistas:
Somos a empresa líder mundial em desenvolvimento de projetos para o mercado
voluntário (VER), gerenciando um portfólio com mais de 50 projetos validados e mais de
16M de toneladas em reduções de emissões;
Pioneira no desenvolvimento de melhores práticas sustentáveis, validamos o primeiro
projeto SOCIALCARBON® em 2008 e o primeiro projeto Gold Standard para o mercado
voluntário no Brasil em 2011;
Mais de 50 comunidades já foram beneficiadas através dos créditos de carbono gerados
para o desenvolvimento sustentável;
Soluções de GEEs para mais de 100 empresas em 23 países.

4. Outros Elementos
Campos de preenchimento opcional
4.1 Informações sobre a performance da organização, em comparação com benchmarks internos (ex: outras
unidades) ou externos (ex: organizações do mesmo setor).
A Sustainable Carbon elabora desde 2012 seu inventário de GEE, e em seu terceiro ano divulgará os resultados no
Registro Público do Programa Brasileiro do GHG Protocol. Foram estabelecidos indicadores de emissões
relacionados às atividades da empresa, visando a redução de emissões e melhoria contínua da coleta e
manipulação dos dados internos. Após analisar as organizações do mesmo setor, foi possível constatar fontes de
emissão semelhantes, porém a Sustainable Carbon reporta algumas fontes de emissão a mais que os benchmarks
externos, como efluentes e resíduos sólidos em escopo 3.
4.2 Descrição de indicadores de emissão de GEE para as atividades da organização. Por exemplo, tCO e/produtos
2
fabricados.
1) O consumo de energia elétrica por m² do escritório foi de 0.012945 tCO2e
2) O consumo de energia elétrica por funcionário do escritório foi de 0.036845 tCO2e
3) A emissão de CO2 por tonelada de redução de emissão de CO2 verificada foi 0,0000171 tCO2e.
4.3 Descrição de estratégias e projetos para a gestão de emissões de GEE.
Uma vez que grande parte das emissões estão alocadas no escopo 3, a Sustainable Carbon trabalha com algumas
medidas e princípios para diminuir as emissões ao longo do tempo, como por exemplo:
Para fazer o transporte de documentos a empresa prefere utilizar os serviços da empresa EcoBike Courier, e com
isso foram evitados 78,59421kg de CO² em São Paulo no ano de 2014.
É pratica comum da empresa: realizar reuniões via vídeo conferencia, evitando assim o deslocamento de ida e volta
até o local da reunião.
Quando realiza visita de verificação dos projetos, opta por fazer a visita de um polo inteiro, passando por todos os
projetos do Estado de uma única vez.
4.4 Informações sobre contratos com clientes e fornecedores que incluam cláusulas vinculadas à elaboração de
inventários de GEE e/ou ao envio de informações relacionadas.
Não foi relatado.
4.5 Informações sobre incertezas, exclusões de fontes de dados e outras características da elaboração do
inventário.
O cálculo de viagens a negócios - escopo 3, foi elaborado em duas frentes. A primeira referente a viagens aéreas e a
segunda em viagens a campo (automóvel), relativo as visitas de verificação dos nossos projetos em diversos polos.
Como descrito na seção 5.3 é pratica comum da empresa é fazer a verificação de todos os projetos do polo em uma
unica verificação. O transporte aéreo realizado fica alocado no cálculo de viagens aéreas. No presente inventário os
dados relativos à quilometragem percorrida no processo de verificação do polo, foram calculados por estimativas
de distância entre os projetos, o que gerou pequeno grau de imprecisão, uma vez que as possíveis rotas para
execução do trajeto são limitadas. Como melhoria na obtenção de dados para relatórios futuros, está sendo
18/09/2019 23:42 10
Inventário 2014 - Sustainable Carbon
implementada a coleta direta a partir do hodômetro no painel do veículo alugado. (Km inicial e final)
4.6 Descrição sobre ações internas para melhoria da qualidade do inventário de GEE. Por exemplo, sistematização
da coleta de dados, contratação de verificação externa, etc.
A Sustainable Carbon desenvolve sistematização de coleta de dados que abrange todo o escopo de suas atividades
organizacionais, através de ferramentas corporativas e a difusão interna do conhecimento sobre as emissões de
GEE.
Para a coleta interna dos dados, criamos uma plataforma com acesso a todos os colaboradores. Este instrumento
possibilita a coleta dos dados assim que ocorre a atividade e sua emissão, dessa forma facilita o monitoramento
dos indicadores, realização do inventário e também o processo de verificação por terceira parte.
Assim como os instrumentos corporativos, o conhecimento compartilhado junto aos colaboradores mostra-se
importante para a qualidade e transparência do inventário. Todos os colaboradores passam por orientações quanto
ao fornecimento dos dados necessários à elaboração do inventário, assim como a realização de treinamentos
internos, visando à difusão de conhecimento sobre os inventários de GEE.
Para obter maior transparência das informações obtidas no inventário, foi estabelecido contrato de prestação de
serviços com uma organização verificadora, acreditada pelo Inmetro, para a validação externa dos dados e
informações do Inventário de GEE.

(denis@sustainablecarbon.com)
Informações institucionais
A Sustainable Carbon é especialista e líder na América Latina no desenvolvimento de
projetos de reduções de emissões e de soluções ligadas ao gerenciamento de Gases de
Efeito Estufa, GEEs.
Nossas soluções permitem que organizações e indivíduos conheçam e reduzam seus
impactos ambientais, possibilitando o equilíbrio das emissões e contribuindo para o
desenvolvimento sustentável de comunidades no mundo todo.
Também conectamos pessoas querendo fazer a diferença no mundo: empresários,
desenvolvedores de projetos, empresas, ONGs, governos, associações e entusiastas de
todo o mundo.
Fazemos isso por meio de mecanismos técnicos e inovadores aprovados pelo mercado de
carbono que quantificam as reduções das emissões GEE. Estabelecendo, assim, um preço
sobre esse serviço ambiental e permitindo o comércio internacional desta commodity
única: o crédito de carbono.
Assim, aqueles que reduzem suas emissões recebem por isso e são capazes de manter os
projetos de carbono, que preservam ecossistemas ameaçados e geram desenvolvimento
na região onde estão localizados.
Somos especialistas no desenvolvimento de projetos de energia renovável, de
substituição de combustível, compostagem, projetos de biomassa e projetos florestais.
Realizamos trabalhos em quase todos os ecossistemas brasileiros. Foi assim que nos
últimos anos a Sustainable Carbon entregou créditos de carbono da mais alta qualidade
para compensações e hoje é a empresa líder neste segmento na América Latina.
Veja abaixo nossas conquistas:
Somos a empresa líder mundial em desenvolvimento de projetos para o mercado
voluntário (VER), gerenciando um portfólio com mais de 50 projetos validados e mais de
16M de toneladas em reduções de emissões;
Pioneira no desenvolvimento de melhores práticas sustentáveis, validamos o primeiro
projeto SOCIALCARBON® em 2008 e o primeiro projeto Gold Standard para o mercado
voluntário no Brasil em 2011;
Mais de 50 comunidades já foram beneficiadas através dos créditos de carbono gerados
para o desenvolvimento sustentável;
Soluções de GEEs para mais de 100 empresas em 23 países.

2
fabricados.
Foram estabelecidos indicadores de emissões relacionados às atividades da empresa, visando a redução de
emissões e melhoria contínua da coleta e manipulação dos dados internos. Os indicadores ligados a consumo de
energia elétrica apresentaram redução significativa em relação ao ano passado, isso porque houve diminuição no
consumo de energia elétrica (cerca de 27% em relação a 2014)
1) O consumo de energia elétrica por m² do escritório foi de 0,009409 tCO2e
2) O consumo de energia elétrica por funcionário do escritório foi de 0,036533 tCO2e
3) A emissão de CO2 por tonelada de redução de emissão de CO2 verificada foi 0,0002448 tCO2e.
4.3 Descrição de estratégias e projetos para a gestão de emissões de GEE.
Uma vez que grande parte das emissões estão alocadas no escopo 3, a Sustainable Carbon trabalha com algumas
medidas e princípios para diminuir as emissões ao longo do tempo, como por exemplo:
Para fazer o transporte de documentos a empresa prefere utilizar os serviços da empresa EcoBike Courier.
É pratica comum da empresa: realizar reuniões via vídeo conferencia, evitando assim o deslocamento de ida e volta
até o local da reunião.
Quando realiza visita de verificação dos projetos, opta por fazer a visita de um polo inteiro, passando por todos os
projetos do Estado de uma única vez.
